title.zh: 卷七十五 眭兩夏侯京翼李傳
title.en: Volume 75: Sui, two Xiahous, Jing, Ji and Li
title.raw: 卷七十五 眭兩夏侯京翼李傳 Volume 75: Sui, two Xiahous, Jing, Ji and Li

sentences.zh: 眭弘字孟，魯國蕃人也。

translations.literal: Sui Hong, courtesy name Meng, was a man of Fan in the state of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Sui Hong, style Meng, came from Fan in the kingdom of Lu.

sentences.zh: 少時好俠，鬥雞走馬，長乃變節，從嬴公受《春秋》。

translations.literal: In youth he loved knight-errantry, cockfighting, and horse racing; when grown he changed his ways and studied the Spring and Autumn Annals under Master Ying.
translations.idiomatic: As a young man he took to bravado, cockfights, and galloping horses; in maturity he changed course and took instruction in the Spring and Autumn Annals from Master Ying.

sentences.zh: 以明經為議郎，至符節令。

translations.literal: By mastery of the classics he became a gentleman consultant and rose to Bearer of the Tallies and Seals.
translations.idiomatic: His classical learning won him appointment as a gentleman consultant; he eventually reached the post of Bearer of Tallies and Seals.

sentences.zh: 孝昭元鳳三年正月，泰山、萊蕪山南匈匈有數千人聲，民視之，有大石自立，高丈五尺，大四十八圍，入地深八尺，三石為足。

translations.literal: In the first month of Emperor Zhao's Yuanfeng third year, south of Mount Tai and Mount Laiwu there was a rumbling like thousands of voices; the people looked and saw a great stone standing upright on its own, one zhang five chi in height, forty-eight wei in girth, eight chi deep in the earth, with three stones as its feet.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the third year of Yuanfeng under Emperor Zhao, south of Mount Tai and Mount Laiwu a sound like thousands of murmuring voices was heard. When people went to look, they found a huge boulder standing upright—fifteen feet tall, forty-eight arm-spans around, sunk eight feet into the ground, propped on three smaller stones like legs.

sentences.zh: 石立後有白烏數千下集其旁。

translations.literal: After the stone stood, several thousand white crows descended and gathered beside it.
translations.idiomatic: After the stone rose, thousands of white crows settled around it.

sentences.zh: 是時，昌邑有枯社木臥復生，又上林苑中大柳樹斷枯臥地，亦自立生，有蟲食樹葉成文字，曰「公孫病已立」，孟推《春秋》之意，以為「石、柳，皆陰類，下民之象；

translations.literal: At this time in Changyi a withered spirit-tree of the earth altar that had lain prone sprouted again; moreover in the Shanglin Park a great willow, broken and withered, lay on the ground yet also stood upright and lived again; there were insects that ate the leaves into characters reading "Gongsun Bingyi shall be established." Meng applied the meaning of the Spring and Autumn Annals and held that "stones and willows are both yin things, images of the common people below."
translations.idiomatic: About the same time in Changyi, a dead ritual tree that had fallen flat at the earth altar put out new growth. In the imperial park at Shanglin, a great willow—snapped off and dry—lay on the ground yet rose and lived again. Insects chewed the leaves into legible words: "Gongsun Bingyi shall rise." Meng read these signs through the Spring and Autumn Annals: stone and willow, he argued, belong to yin; they stand for the people beneath the throne.

sentences.zh: 泰山者，岱宗之岳，王者易姓告代之外。

translations.literal: Mount Tai is the peak of Dai Zong; it is the place outside of which kings who change the surname and announce the succession make their proclamation.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Tai is the sacred height of Dai Zong—the ground where a new dynasty proclaims the change of mandate.

sentences.zh: 今大石自立，僵柳復起，非人力所為，此當有從匹夫為天子者。

translations.literal: Now a great stone has stood of itself and a dead willow has risen again; this is not the work of human power—there ought to be one who from a common man becomes Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A boulder that stands by itself and a dead willow that lives again are beyond human contrivance. They point to someone of no rank who will mount the throne.

sentences.zh: 枯社木復生，故廢之家公孫氏當復興者也。」

translations.literal: The withered spirit-tree of the earth altar sprouting again means the house of Gongsun, formerly cast down, ought to rise again."
translations.idiomatic: The dead altar tree coming back to life means the fallen Gongsun line is destined to flourish again."

sentences.zh: 孟意亦不知其所在，即說曰：「先師董仲舒有言，雖有繼體守文之君，不害聖人之受命。

translations.literal: Meng also did not know where he was; then he explained, saying, "My late teacher Dong Zhongshu said that even when there is a successor who keeps to the written norm, it does not hinder a sage from receiving the mandate."
translations.idiomatic: Meng had no idea where this heir might be found, but he argued as follows: "My teacher Dong Zhongshu taught that even a legitimate heir who merely preserves the letter of the law cannot block a sage from receiving Heaven's mandate."

sentences.zh: 漢家堯後，有傳國之運。

translations.literal: The house of Han is descended from Yao; it has the fortune to transmit the state.
translations.idiomatic: The Han house traces its line to Yao; it was granted the fortune to hand the realm down in succession.

sentences.zh: 漢帝宜誰差天下，求索賢人，禪以帝位，而退自封百里，如殷、周二王后，以承順天命。」

translations.literal: The Han emperor ought to search the realm in orderly fashion, seek out a worthy man, yield the imperial throne to him, and in retirement enfeoff himself with a hundred li, like the two royal houses after Yin and Zhou, thereby to comply with Heaven's command."
translations.idiomatic: The Han emperor should cast the net across the empire, find a worthy successor, abdicate in his favor, and retire to a modest fief of his own—on the model of the two royal houses that survived after Shang and Zhou—so as to answer Heaven's will."

sentences.zh: 孟使友人內官長賜上此書。

translations.literal: Meng had his friend, the palace internuncio named Ci, submit this memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Meng had his friend Ci, chief of the inner attendants, present the memorial.

sentences.zh: 時，昭帝幼，大將軍霍光秉政，惡之，下其書廷尉。

translations.literal: At the time Emperor Zhao was young; Grand General Huo Guang held power, detested it, and referred the memorial to the commandant of justice.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhao was still a child; Grand General Huo Guang ran the government. He took offense and sent the document to the commandant of justice.

sentences.zh: 奏賜、孟妄設襖言惑眾，大逆不道，皆伏誅。

translations.literal: It was reported that Ci and Meng had wantonly set forth heterodox words to delude the multitude, committing great treason against the Way; both were executed.
translations.idiomatic: The court ruled that Ci and Meng had spread occult nonsense to mislead the people—capital treason. Both were put to death.

sentences.zh: 後五年，孝宣帝興於民間，即位，征孟子為郎。

translations.literal: After five years, Emperor Xuan arose from among the common people; when he ascended the throne, he summoned Meng's son and made him a gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later Emperor Xuan emerged from obscurity among the people. Once enthroned, he called up Meng's son and gave him a post in the palace corps.

sentences.zh: 夏侯始昌，魯人也。

translations.literal: Xiahou Shichang was a man of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Xiahou Shichang came from Lu.

sentences.zh: 通《五經》，以《齊詩》、《尚書》教授。

translations.literal: He mastered the Five Classics and taught the Qi version of the Odes and the Documents.
translations.idiomatic: He was versed in the Five Classics and lectured on the Qi recension of the Odes and on the Documents.

sentences.zh: 自董仲舒、韓嬰死後，武帝得始昌，甚重之。

translations.literal: After Dong Zhongshu and Han Ying died, when Emperor Wu obtained Shichang he greatly esteemed him.
translations.idiomatic: After Dong Zhongshu and Han Ying were gone, Emperor Wu prized Shichang as soon as he came to court.

sentences.zh: 始昌明於陰陽，先言柏梁台災曰，至期日果災。

translations.literal: Shichang was clear on yin and yang; he first stated the day of the Boliang Terrace disaster, and when the appointed day came there was indeed a disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Shichang excelled at yin-and-yang lore. He predicted the day the Boliang Terrace would burn, and on that very day the fire came.

sentences.zh: 時，昌邑王以少子愛，上為選師，始昌為太傅。

translations.literal: At the time the king of Changyi, as a younger son, was favored; the emperor selected a tutor for him, and Shichang became grand tutor.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Changyi was the emperor's beloved younger son, so the throne chose him a teacher: Shichang became his grand tutor.

sentences.zh: 年老，以壽終。

translations.literal: In old age he ended his life by the full span of years.
translations.idiomatic: He died in advanced age, having lived out his years.

sentences.zh: 族子勝亦以儒顯名。

translations.literal: His clansman's son Sheng also became illustrious in name through Confucian learning.
translations.idiomatic: A nephew by clan, Sheng, likewise won fame as a Confucian scholar.

sentences.zh: 夏侯勝字長公。

translations.literal: Xiahou Sheng, courtesy name Zhanggong.
translations.idiomatic: Xiahou Sheng, style Zhanggong.

sentences.zh: 初，魯共王分魯西寧鄉以封子節侯，別屬大河，大河後更名東平，故勝為東平人。

translations.literal: Originally the Prince Gong of Lu divided off Ning township west of Lu to enfeoff his son as marquis of Jie; it was separately attached to Great River commandery, which later was renamed Dongping; therefore Sheng was a man of Dongping.
translations.idiomatic: Long before, the Prince Gong of Lu had carved out Ning township west of Lu for his son, the Marquis of Jie. The fief lay under Great River commandery, later renamed Dongping—which is why Sheng counted as a man of Dongping.

sentences.zh: 勝少孤，好學，從始昌受《尚書》及《洪範五行傳》，說災異。

translations.literal: Sheng was orphaned young, loved learning, studied the Documents and the Hong fan wuxing zhuan under Shichang, and discoursed on portents and anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: Orphaned while young, Sheng loved books. He took the Documents and the treatise on the Five Phases in the "Great Plan" from Shichang and made a specialty of interpreting omens.

sentences.zh: 後事□卿，又從歐陽氏問。

translations.literal: Afterward he served a minister [name missing in text] and also questioned the Ouyang school.
translations.idiomatic: He later entered the service of a high minister—the graph for the office is lost in the text—and pursued further study with the Ouyang masters.

sentences.zh: 為學精孰，所問非一師也。

translations.literal: In his learning he was refined and thorough; those he questioned were not from one teacher alone.
translations.idiomatic: His scholarship was deep and exacting, and he did not rely on a single teacher.

sentences.zh: 善說禮服。

translations.literal: He was good at explaining ritual and mourning garments.
translations.idiomatic: He was an authority on ritual and the gradations of mourning dress.

sentences.zh: 徵為博士、光祿大夫。

translations.literal: He was summoned as an erudite and as supernumerary grandee of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The court called him up as an erudite and then as supernumerary grandee of the palace.

sentences.zh: 會昭帝崩，昌邑王嗣立，數出。

translations.literal: When Emperor Zhao died, the king of Changyi succeeded and went out frequently.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhao died; the king of Changyi took the throne and began riding out of the palace again and again.

sentences.zh: 勝當乘輿前諫曰：「天久陰而不雨，臣下有謀上者，陛下出欲何之？」

translations.literal: Sheng stood before the imperial carriage and remonstrated, saying, "Heaven has long been overcast and does not rain; among the ministers there is one who plots against the sovereign—where does Your Majesty intend to go when you go out?"
translations.idiomatic: Sheng stepped in front of the royal chariot and warned him: "The sky has stayed gray for days without rain—an omen that someone below is plotting against the throne. Where do you mean to ride, Your Majesty?"

sentences.zh: 王怒，謂勝為襖言，縛以屬吏。

translations.literal: The king was angry, called Sheng's words heterodox, and bound him over to the clerks.
translations.idiomatic: The king flew into a rage, denounced Sheng's words as occult nonsense, and had him bound and handed to the magistrates.

sentences.zh: 吏白大將軍霍光，光不舉法。

translations.literal: The clerks reported to Grand General Huo Guang; Guang did not apply the law.
translations.idiomatic: The officials reported the matter to Grand General Huo Guang, who declined to press charges.

sentences.zh: 是時，光與車騎將軍張安世謀欲廢昌邑王。

translations.literal: At this time Guang and chariot-and-cavalry general Zhang Anshi were plotting to depose the king of Changyi.
translations.idiomatic: By then Huo Guang and Chariot-and-Cavalry General Zhang Anshi were already planning to remove the king of Changyi.

sentences.zh: 光讓安世以為洩語，安世實不言。

translations.literal: Guang blamed Anshi, thinking he had leaked the words; Anshi in fact had not spoken.
translations.idiomatic: Guang suspected Anshi of having leaked their secret; Anshi had said nothing of the kind.

sentences.zh: 乃召問勝，勝對言：「在《洪範傳》曰『皇之不極，厥罰常陰，時則下人有伐上者』，惡察察言，故云臣下有謀。」

translations.literal: Then he summoned and questioned Sheng; Sheng replied, saying, "In the Hong fan zhuan it says, 'When the sovereign does not reach the utmost, the punishment thereof is constant overcast; then there are those below who attack those above'—I disliked speaking plainly, therefore I said ministers below plot."
translations.idiomatic: Guang had Sheng brought in. Sheng answered: "The commentary on the 'Great Plan' reads, 'When the ruler fails the supreme norm, Heaven's penalty is endless gloom—then subjects rise against their lord.' I softened the wording because I did not wish to speak too bluntly; that is why I spoke only of ministers plotting."

sentences.zh: 光、安世大驚，以此益重經術士。

translations.literal: Guang and Anshi were greatly startled; because of this they esteemed classicists all the more.
translations.idiomatic: Guang and Anshi were stunned—and from that day they held men of classical learning in even higher regard.

sentences.zh: 後十餘日，光卒與安世白太后，廢昌邑王，尊立宣帝。

translations.literal: After more than ten days Guang finally, with Anshi, reported to the empress dowager, deposed the king of Changyi, and honored and established Emperor Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: A little over ten days later Huo Guang and Zhang Anshi went to the empress dowager, secured the deposition of the king of Changyi, and raised Emperor Xuan to the throne.

sentences.zh: 光以為群臣奏事東宮，太后省政，宜知經術，白令勝用《尚書》授太后。

translations.literal: Guang thought that when the host of ministers presented affairs in the Eastern Palace and the empress dowager examined government, she ought to know the classics; he reported and ordered Sheng to instruct the empress dowager with the Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Guang reasoned that ministers reporting to the Eastern Palace and the empress dowager overseeing the realm ought to be guided by the classics. He arranged for Sheng to teach her the Documents.

sentences.zh: 遷長信少府，賜爵關內侯，以與謀廢立，定策安宗廟，益千戶。

translations.literal: He was promoted to steward of Changxin, ennobled as marquis within the passes, and for participating in the plot of deposition and establishment, settling the plan and securing the ancestral temples, his fief was increased by a thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng was promoted to steward of the Changxin Palace and ennobled as a marquis within the passes for his part in the coup that set the succession and steadied the imperial shrines; his income was raised by a thousand households.

sentences.zh: 勝、霸既久系，霸欲從勝受經，勝辭以罪死。

translations.literal: After Sheng and Ba had long been imprisoned, Ba wished to receive the classics from Sheng; Sheng declined on the ground that he was guilty and faced death.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng and Huang Ba had been in chains a long time when Ba asked to study the classics under him. Sheng refused: he was a condemned man awaiting execution.

sentences.zh: 霸曰：「『朝聞道，夕死可矣』。」

translations.literal: Ba said, "'If in the morning I have heard the Way, in the evening I may die'—that is enough."
translations.idiomatic: Ba replied, "The Master said, 'Hear the Way in the morning, and dying that evening is no regret.'"

sentences.zh: 勝賢其言，遂授之。

translations.literal: Sheng deemed his words worthy and thereupon taught him.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng judged the sentiment noble and took him on as a pupil.

sentences.zh: 系再更冬，講論不怠。

translations.literal: Imprisoned through two changes of winter, they lectured and discussed without slackening.
translations.idiomatic: They remained jailed through two winters, yet never broke off their lessons and debate.

sentences.zh: 至四年夏，關東四十九郡同日地動，或山崩，壞城郭室屋，殺六千餘人。

translations.literal: When it came to the summer of the fourth year, forty-nine commanderies east of the Pass on the same day had earthquakes; in some cases mountains collapsed, destroying city walls, houses, and chambers, and killing more than six thousand people.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of the fourth year, forty-nine commanderies east of the Pass were shaken on the same day. Mountains gave way, cities and homes were smashed, and more than six thousand people perished.

sentences.zh: 上乃素服，避正殿，遣使者弔問吏民，賜死者棺錢。

translations.literal: The emperor thereupon wore plain garb, avoided the main hall, sent envoys to condole with officials and commoners, and granted coffin money to the dead.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor put on mourning white, quit the main hall, sent out envoys to comfort officials and commoners alike, and paid stipends toward the burial of the dead.

sentences.zh: 下詔曰：「蓋災異者，天地之戒也。

translations.literal: An edict was issued, saying, "Portents and anomalies are the warnings of Heaven and Earth.
translations.idiomatic: He issued an edict: "Portents are Heaven and Earth warning the throne.

sentences.zh: 朕承洪業，托士民之上，未能和群生。

translations.literal: We inherit the great enterprise and stand above officers and people, yet have been unable to harmonize the multitude of lives.
translations.idiomatic: I have inherited a vast charge and stand above the realm, yet I have failed to bring peace to the people.

sentences.zh: 曩者地震北海、琅邪，壞祖宗廟，朕甚懼焉。

translations.literal: Formerly there were earthquakes in Beihai and Langye that destroyed the temples of the ancestors; We are greatly fearful thereof.
translations.idiomatic: Not long ago the earth shook in Beihai and Langye and shattered the shrines of my forebears. I am terrified at the thought.

sentences.zh: 其與列侯、中二千石博問術士，有以應變，補朕之闕，毋有所諱。」

translations.literal: Let them together with the full marquises and those of two thousand piculs at the middle rank broadly question specialists in the arts, that there may be means to respond to change and remedy Our deficiencies—let there be nothing concealed."
translations.idiomatic: Let the full marquises and all ministers at the two-thousand-picul level cast widely for men of learning who can read these signs and tell me how to mend my rule. Speak freely; hold nothing back."

sentences.zh: 因大赦。

translations.literal: Thereupon there was a great amnesty.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty followed.

sentences.zh: 勝出為諫大夫、給事中，霸為揚州剌吏。

translations.literal: Sheng came out and became grandee remonstrant and attendant at the palace; Ba became inspector of Yang province.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng was released and appointed grandee remonstrant with standing as palace attendant; Huang Ba was sent out as inspector of Yang province.

sentences.zh: 勝為人質樸守正，簡易亡威儀。

translations.literal: In his person Sheng was plain and upright in keeping to the correct, simple and easy-going, without imposing dignity.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng was blunt, honest, and informal—he carried none of the courtier's studied gravitas.

sentences.zh: 見時謂上為君，誤相字於前，上亦以是親信之。

translations.literal: When received in audience he called the sovereign "lord" by mistake and used the familiar "you" before him; the sovereign for this reason was intimate with and trusted him.
translations.idiomatic: In audience he once addressed the emperor as "my lord" and slipped into the familiar "you" to his face; the emperor took it as a mark of sincerity and drew him close.

sentences.zh: 嘗見，出道上語，上聞而讓勝，勝曰：「陛下所言善，臣故揚之。

translations.literal: Once when he had been received in audience, leaving he repeated the sovereign's words on the road; when the sovereign heard of it he reproached Sheng, and Sheng said, "What Your Majesty said was good; your servant therefore spread it.
translations.idiomatic: After one audience he repeated the emperor's remarks in the street. When word got back, the emperor rebuked him. Sheng replied, "Your Majesty spoke well; I was only giving your words wider currency."

sentences.zh: 堯言佈於天下，至今見誦。

translations.literal: What Yao said was published throughout the realm and to this day is recited.
translations.idiomatic: Yao's sayings were broadcast across the realm and people still quote them today.

sentences.zh: 臣以為可傳，故傳耳。」

translations.literal: Your servant thought it could be transmitted, therefore transmitted it."
translations.idiomatic: I thought them worth passing on, so I passed them on."

sentences.zh: 朝廷每有大議，上知勝素直，謂曰：「先生通正言，無懲前事。」

translations.literal: Whenever the court had great deliberations, the sovereign knew Sheng was by nature straight and said to him, "Sir, speak straightforwardly; do not fear past affairs."
translations.idiomatic: Whenever weighty policy was debated, the emperor, knowing Sheng's blunt honesty, would tell him, "Speak plainly, sir; do not mind what happened before."

sentences.zh: 勝復為長信少府，遷太子太傅。

translations.literal: Sheng again became steward of Changxin and was promoted to grand tutor of the heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng returned to the stewardship of Changxin and was raised to grand tutor of the heir apparent.

sentences.zh: 受詔撰《尚書》、《論語說》，賜黃金百斤。

translations.literal: He received an order to compose treatises on the Documents and the Analects and was granted a hundred catties of gold.
translations.idiomatic: He was commissioned to write expository works on the Documents and the Analects and received a gift of a hundred catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 年九十卒官，賜塚塋，葬平陵。

translations.literal: At age ninety he died in office; he was granted a tomb precinct and was buried at Pingling.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office at ninety and was granted a burial plot at Pingling.

sentences.zh: 太后賜錢二百萬，為勝素服五日，以報師傅之恩，儒者以為榮。

translations.literal: The empress dowager granted two million cash, wore plain mourning for Sheng for five days to repay the kindness of a tutor, and the Confucians deemed it an honor.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager sent two million cash, wore mourning for him five days in return for his service as her teacher—a gesture the scholars counted among the highest honors.

sentences.zh: 始，勝每講授，常謂諸生曰：「士病不明經術，經術苟明，其取青紫如俯拾地芥耳。

translations.literal: At first, whenever Sheng lectured, he always said to the students, "The gentleman's fault is not to clarify the classics; if the classics are clarified, taking the blue and purple is like stooping to pick mustard from the ground.
translations.idiomatic: In his teaching Sheng often told his pupils, "A scholar's worst failing is not to master the canon. Once the canon is clear, high office is yours for the stooping—it is like plucking greens from the ground."

sentences.zh: 學經不明，不如歸耕。」

translations.literal: If one studies the classics and does not clarify them, it is better to return to plowing."
translations.idiomatic: If you cannot make sense of the classics, you might as well go home and farm."

sentences.zh: 勝從父子建字長卿，自師事勝及歐陽高，左右采獲，又從《五經》諸儒問與《尚書》相出入者，牽引以次章句，具文飾說。

translations.literal: Sheng's nephew by a younger brother of the same grandfather, Jian, courtesy name Zhangqing, took Sheng and Ouyang Gao as his teachers, gathered from right and left, and also questioned the Confucians of the Five Classics on what diverged from or agreed with the Documents, drawing them in to arrange the glosses in order and fully ornament the explanations.
translations.idiomatic: Jian—Sheng's grand-nephew on the younger line—style Zhangqing, studied under both Sheng and Ouyang Gao, cast his net wide among the Five Classics, and wove every gloss that touched the Documents into an orderly, elaborately argued system.

sentences.zh: 勝非之曰：「建所謂章句小儒，破碎大道。」

translations.literal: Sheng condemned him, saying, "What Jian calls is the petty Confucianism of glosses that shatters the great Way."
translations.idiomatic: Sheng dismissed his work: "That is the pedantry of line-by-line glosses—it splinters the great Way."

sentences.zh: 建亦非勝為學疏略，難以應敵。

translations.literal: Jian also condemned Sheng for learning that was cursory and hard to use against opponents.
translations.idiomatic: Jian shot back that Sheng's scholarship was too loose to stand up in debate.

sentences.zh: 建卒自顓門名經，為議郎、博士，至太子少傅。

translations.literal: Jian in the end became illustrious in his own gate for mastery of the classics, became gentleman consultant and erudite, and reached junior tutor to the heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: Jian nonetheless made a name as a classicist in his own school, rose to gentleman consultant and erudite, and ended as junior tutor to the heir apparent.

sentences.zh: 勝子兼為左曹太中大夫，孫堯至長信少府、司農、鴻臚，曾孫蕃郡守、州牧、長樂少府。

translations.literal: Sheng's son Jian became senior clerk of the left and grandee of the palace; his grandson Yao reached steward of Changxin, minister of agriculture, and herald; his great-grandson Fan was commandery governor, provincial shepherd, and junior steward of Changle.
translations.idiomatic: His son Jian rose to senior clerk of the left and grandee of the palace; his grandson Yao served as steward of Changxin, minister of agriculture, and herald; a great-grandson, Fan, governed commanderies and provinces and became junior steward of Changle.

sentences.zh: 勝同產弟子賞為梁內史，梁內史子定國為豫章太守。

translations.literal: Sheng's full brother's son Shang became inner governor of Liang; the inner governor of Liang's son Dingguo became governor of Yuzhang.
translations.idiomatic: A son of his full brother, Shang, became inner governor of Liang; Shang's son Dingguo held Yuzhang as governor.

sentences.zh: 而建子千秋亦為少府、太子少傅。

translations.literal: And Jian's son Qianqiu also became junior steward and junior tutor to the heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: Jian's son Qianqiu likewise reached junior steward and junior tutor to the heir apparent.

sentences.zh: 京房字君明，東郡頓丘人也。

translations.literal: Jing Fang, courtesy name Junming, was a man of Dunqiu in Dong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang, style Junming, came from Dunqiu in Dong commandery.

sentences.zh: 治《易》，事梁人焦延壽。

translations.literal: He studied the Changes and served the man of Liang, Jiao Yanshou.
translations.idiomatic: He took the Book of Changes from Jiao Yanshou of Liang.

sentences.zh: 延壽字贛。

translations.literal: Yanshou's courtesy name was Gan.
translations.idiomatic: Yanshou's style name was Gan.

sentences.zh: 贛貧賤，以好學得幸梁王。

translations.literal: Gan was poor and lowly; by love of learning he won the favor of the king of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Though poor and obscure, Gan won the king of Liang's favor through sheer devotion to study.

sentences.zh: 梁王共其資用，令極意學。

translations.literal: The king of Liang supplied his expenses and ordered him to pursue learning to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Liang paid his bills and told him to spare no effort in his studies.

sentences.zh: 既成，為郡史，察舉補小黃令。

translations.literal: When his training was complete, he became a clerk of the commandery, was recommended and appointed magistrate of Xiaohuang.
translations.idiomatic: When his training was done, he became a commandery clerk, then was recommended and named magistrate of Xiaohuang.

sentences.zh: 以候司先知奸邪，盜賊不得發。

translations.literal: By observing signs he knew beforehand of wickedness and treachery, so thieves and bandits could not arise.
translations.idiomatic: By reading omens he foreknew crime and treachery, so thieves never got a foothold in his district.

sentences.zh: 愛養吏民，化行縣中。

translations.literal: He cherished and nurtured clerks and commoners; his transforming influence spread through the county.
translations.idiomatic: He cared for his officials and the people, and his moral influence spread through the county.

sentences.zh: 舉最當遷，三老官屬上書願留贛，有詔許增秩留，卒於小黃。

translations.literal: Recommended as foremost he ought to have been transferred; the elders and subordinate officials submitted a memorial asking to retain Gan; there was an edict permitting increase of rank and retention, and he ended his life at Xiaohuang.
translations.idiomatic: Rated top in the circuit, he was due for promotion, but the elders and his staff petitioned to keep him; the throne raised his rank and let him stay, and he died in office at Xiaohuang.

sentences.zh: 贛常曰：「得我道以亡身者，必京生也。」

translations.literal: Gan always said, "He who obtains my Way and loses his body will surely be Master Jing."
translations.idiomatic: Gan used to say, "The disciple who masters my teaching and pays for it with his life will be Jing."

sentences.zh: 其說長於災變，分六十四卦，更直日用事，以風雨寒溫為候：各有占驗。

translations.literal: His doctrine excelled in disasters and anomalies; he divided the sixty-four hexagrams and rotated them to preside over the days, taking wind, rain, cold, and warmth as signs: each had its prognostications.
translations.idiomatic: His specialty was portents: he mapped the sixty-four hexagrams to the calendar, assigning each day its hexagram and reading wind, rain, cold, and warmth as omens, each with its rule of interpretation.

sentences.zh: 房用之尤精。

translations.literal: Fang applied it with particular refinement.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang applied this method with exceptional skill.

sentences.zh: 好鐘律，知音聲。

translations.literal: He loved bells and pitch pipes and understood musical sounds.
translations.idiomatic: He had a passion for acoustics and pitch pipes and understood tone.

sentences.zh: 初元四年以孝廉為郎。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Chuyuan he became a gentleman by filial integrity and incorruptibility.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Chuyuan he entered the palace corps on a recommendation for filial piety and clean conduct.

sentences.zh: 永光、建昭間，西羌反，日蝕，又久青亡光，陰霧不精。

translations.literal: Between Yongguang and Jianzhao the Western Qiang rebelled, there was a solar eclipse, and moreover for a long time the firmament was without light, yin mists impure.
translations.idiomatic: During Yongguang and Jianzhao the Western Qiang rose, the sun was eclipsed, and for long stretches the sky stayed dim under a haze that would not lift.

sentences.zh: 房數上疏，先言其將然，近數月，遠一歲，所言屢中，天子說之。

translations.literal: Fang repeatedly submitted memorials, first stating what would happen; near at hand several months, far off one year—what he said repeatedly hit the mark, and the Son of Heaven was pleased with him.
translations.idiomatic: Fang sent memorial after memorial, predicting events weeks or a year ahead, and he was right so often that the emperor took a liking to him.

sentences.zh: 數召見問，房對曰：「古帝王以功舉賢，則萬化成，瑞應著，末世以毀譽取人，故功業廢而致災異。

translations.literal: He was repeatedly summoned and questioned; Fang replied, saying, "The ancient emperors and kings raised the worthy by merit, then the myriad transformations were accomplished and auspicious responses appeared; in a degenerate age they took men by slander and praise, therefore merit was abandoned and portents and anomalies arose.
translations.idiomatic: Summoned repeatedly for counsel, Fang said: "The sage-kings of old chose ministers on merit, and the realm responded with good omens; late ages pick favorites by gossip, and that is why government fails and Heaven sends warnings."

sentences.zh: 宜令百官各試其功，災異可息。

translations.literal: It is fitting to order the hundred offices each to try their merit; then portents and anomalies can be stilled.
translations.idiomatic: Let every office be examined on its performance—then these omens will subside.

sentences.zh: 詔使房作其事，房奏考功課吏法。

translations.literal: An edict ordered Fang to carry this out; Fang presented the law for examining merit and regulating officials.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor told him to put the idea into practice; Fang drafted a statute for grading officials by results.

sentences.zh: 上令公卿朝臣與房會議溫室，皆以房言煩碎，令上下相司，不可許。

translations.literal: The sovereign ordered the dukes, ministers, and courtiers to meet with Fang in the hothouse; all held Fang's words vexatiously minute, with superiors and subordinates spying on each other, and said it could not be permitted.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had the high ministers meet Fang in the greenhouse; they dismissed his scheme as petty mutual surveillance and voted it down.

sentences.zh: 上意鄉之。

translations.literal: The sovereign's mind inclined toward it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor himself still leaned toward Fang's plan.

sentences.zh: 時，部刺史奏事京師，上召見諸刺史，令房曉以課事，刺史復以為不可行。

translations.literal: At the time the regional inspectors were presenting affairs in the capital; the sovereign summoned and saw the various inspectors and ordered Fang to explain to them the examination matter; the inspectors again held it unworkable.
translations.idiomatic: When the regional inspectors came to the capital, the emperor assembled them and had Fang explain the grading system; they too said it would not work.

sentences.zh: 唯御史大夫鄭私、光祿大夫周堪初言不可，後善之。

translations.literal: Only imperial counsellor Zheng Si and supernumerary grandee of the palace Zhou Kan at first said it could not be done, afterward approved it.
translations.idiomatic: Only Imperial Counsellor Zheng Si and Supernumerary Grandee Zhou Kan opposed it at first, then came around.

sentences.zh: 房罷出，後上令房上弟子曉知考功課吏事者，欲試用之。

translations.literal: Fang having been dismissed and gone out, afterward the sovereign ordered Fang to present disciples who understood the matter of examining merit and regulating officials, wishing to try employing them.
translations.idiomatic: After Fang left court, the emperor asked him to name pupils who understood his grading system, intending to test the idea through them.

sentences.zh: 房上中郎任良、姚平，「願以為刺史，試考功法，臣得通籍殿中，為奏事，以防雍塞。」

translations.literal: Fang presented gentlemen of the palace Ren Liang and Yao Ping: "I beg to make them regional inspectors and try the method of examining merit; your servant may have registry access within the hall and present memorials to prevent blockage."
translations.idiomatic: He nominated Ren Liang and Yao Ping of the palace corps: "Appoint them inspectors and trial the merit law; grant me standing in the hall to memorialize so nothing is blocked."

sentences.zh: 石顯、五鹿充宗皆疾房，欲遠之，建言宜試以房為郡守。

translations.literal: Shi Xian and Wulu Chongzong both hated Fang and wished to distance him; they proposed that it was fitting to try making Fang a commandery governor.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Xian and Wulu Chongzong detested Fang and wanted him out of the capital; they urged the throne to send him out as a commandery governor.

sentences.zh: 元帝於是以房為魏郡太守，秩八百石居，得以考功法治郡。

translations.literal: Emperor Yuan thereupon made Fang governor of Wei commandery at rank eight hundred piculs residence, enabling him to govern the commandery with the merit-examining method.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Yuan named him governor of Wei at eight hundred piculs' salary, with authority to run the commandery by the new merit rules.

sentences.zh: 房自請，願無屬刺史，得除用它郡人，自第吏千石已下，歲竟乘傳奏事。

translations.literal: Fang requested on his own that he not be subordinate to the regional inspector, that he be permitted to appoint men from other commanderies below a thousand piculs, and that at year's end he ride the post relay to present affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Fang asked to report directly without answering to the regional inspector, to pick his own subordinates below the thousand-picul grade from other commanderies, and to ride the post relays to the capital at year's end with his accounts.

sentences.zh: 天子許焉。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven granted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved his request.

sentences.zh: 房自知數以論議為大臣所非，內與石顯、五鹿充宗有隙，不欲遠離左右，及為太守，憂懼。

translations.literal: Fang himself knew that repeatedly in disputation he had been rejected by the great ministers; inwardly he had a rift with Shi Xian and Wulu Chongzong and did not wish to be far from the ruler's side; when he became a grand governor he was anxious and fearful.
translations.idiomatic: Fang knew his policy debates had earned the enmity of the high ministers, and that Shi Xian and Wulu Chongzong were set against him; he had wanted to stay near the throne, and once he was sent out as a governor, dread took hold of him.

sentences.zh: 房以建昭二年二月朔拜，上封事曰：「辛酉已來，蒙氣衰去，太陽精明，臣獨欣然，以為陛下有所定也。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianzhao, on the new moon of the second month, Fang took office and submitted a sealed memorial, saying, "Since xinyou the shrouding vapor has waned and departed, the great yang is clear and bright; your servant alone rejoiced, thinking Your Majesty had settled on a course."
translations.idiomatic: He was invested on the new moon of the second month, Jianzhao 2, and at once sent up a sealed note: "After the xinyou day the murky haze lifted and the imperial sun shone clear—I dared hope Your Majesty had made up your mind."

sentences.zh: 然少陰倍力而乘消息。

translations.literal: Yet lesser yin doubles its force and rides upon the waxing and waning.
translations.idiomatic: But minor yin has redoubled and rides the cycle of rise and fall—Heaven's balance is still unsettled.

sentences.zh: 臣疑陛下雖行此道，猶不得如意，臣竊悼懼。

translations.literal: Your servant suspects that although Your Majesty walks this path, you may still not obtain your wish; your servant privately mourns and fears.
translations.idiomatic: I fear that even if you follow this course, you may not get what you seek—I grieve and tremble at the thought.

sentences.zh: 守陽平侯鳳欲見未得，至己卯，臣拜為太守，此言上雖明下猶勝之效也。

translations.literal: Marquis of Yangping Feng wished to see but did not obtain it; when it came to jimao, your servant was appointed grand governor—this shows that although those above are clear, those below still prevail in the effect.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Feng of Yangping tried to see me and could not; by jimao I had my commission as governor—proof that clarity above does not stop malice below from carrying the day.

sentences.zh: 臣出之後，恐必為用事所蔽，身死而功不成，故願歲盡乘傳奏事，蒙哀見許。

translations.literal: After your servant goes out, I fear I shall surely be blocked by those in power, my body dead and merit unaccomplished; therefore I beg that at year's end I may ride the post relay and present affairs, and that in compassion I be permitted.
translations.idiomatic: Once I leave the capital, the faction in power will smother me—I may die before anything is achieved. I beg leave to ride the post stages at year's end and report to you; grant me that mercy.

sentences.zh: 乃辛巳，蒙氣復乘卦，太陽侵色，此上大夫覆陽而上意疑也。

translations.literal: Then on xinsi the shrouding vapor again rode the hexagrams, the great yang suffered encroachment on its color—this is great ministers overturning yang and the sovereign's mind growing doubtful.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi the veil closed over the omens again and the sovereign sun dimmed—high ministers are eclipsing yang and sowing doubt in the ruler's heart.

sentences.zh: 已卯、庚辰之間，必有欲隔絕臣令不得乘傳奏事者。」

translations.literal: Between jimao and gengchen there must be one who wishes to cut off your servant and make him unable to ride the post relay and present affairs."
translations.idiomatic: Between jimao and gengchen someone will move to cut me off from the post roads and keep my memorials from reaching you."

sentences.zh: 房未發，上令陽平侯鳳承製詔房，止無乘傳奏事。

translations.literal: Before Fang had set out, the sovereign ordered Marquis of Yangping Feng to receive the edict in form and inform Fang to desist and not ride the post relay to present affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Fang had not yet left when the emperor had Marquis Feng of Yangping deliver an edict: he was to stop asking for post-stage access to memorialize.

sentences.zh: 房意愈恐，去至新豐，因郵上封事曰：「臣前以六月中言《遁卦》不效，法曰：『道人始去，寒，湧水為災。』

translations.literal: Fang's mind grew more fearful; when he had gone as far as Xinfeng he submitted by post a sealed memorial, saying, "Your servant previously in the sixth month said the Retiring hexagram would not verify; the rule reads, 'When the man of the Way first departs, there is cold; surging water is the disaster.'"
translations.idiomatic: His fear deepened. At Xinfeng he sent another sealed memorial by the courier post: "In the sixth month I said the Retiring hexagram would fail its sign; the canon runs, 'When the Wayfarer first goes forth, there is cold—boiling flood is the calamity.'"

sentences.zh: 至其七月，湧水出。

translations.literal: When it came to the seventh month, surging water appeared.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the floods came, just as foretold.

sentences.zh: 臣弟子姚平謂臣曰：『房可謂知道，未可謂信道也。

translations.literal: Your disciple Yao Ping said to your servant, 'Fang may be said to know the Way; he may not be said to trust the Way.
translations.idiomatic: My pupil Yao Ping told me, 'You may know the Way, Master, but you do not yet trust it.

sentences.zh: 房言災異，未嘗不中，今湧水已出，道人當遂死，尚復何言？』

translations.literal: Fang's words on portents and anomalies have never missed; now surging water has already appeared—the man of the Way ought therefore to die; what more is there to say?'
translations.idiomatic: Your predictions have never failed; the waters have risen—the Wayfarer should accept death. What is left to say?'

sentences.zh: 臣曰：『陛下至仁，於臣尤厚，雖言而死，臣猶言也。』

translations.literal: Your servant said, 'Your Majesty is supremely benevolent and toward your servant especially generous; although I speak and die, your servant still will speak.'
translations.idiomatic: I answered, 'The Son of Heaven is utmost in kindness and has shown me singular favor; I will speak though it cost my life.'

sentences.zh: 平又曰：『房可謂小忠，未可謂大忠也。

translations.literal: Ping again said, 'Fang may be called small in loyalty; he may not be called great in loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: Ping went on: 'That is petty loyalty, not the great sort.

sentences.zh: 昔秦時趙高用事，有正先者，非刺高而死，高威自此成，故秦之亂，正先趣之。』

translations.literal: In former times when Zhao Gao held power in Qin, there was one Zheng Xian who criticized Gao and died for it; from this Gao's awesomeness was achieved—therefore the disorder of Qin, Zheng Xian hastened it.'
translations.idiomatic: In the Qin days Zhao Gao ruled the court; Zheng Xian spoke against him and was killed for it, and Zhao Gao's terror dated from that hour—the ruin of Qin, Zheng Xian sped it on.'

sentences.zh: 今臣得出守郡，自詭效功，恐未效而死。

translations.literal: Now your servant is able to go out and guard a commandery, falsely promising to show merit; I fear I may die before merit is shown.
translations.idiomatic: Now I am sent to govern a county on a boast that I will deliver results—I may die before a single result appears.

sentences.zh: 惟陛下毋使臣塞湧水之異，當正先之死，為姚平所笑。」

translations.literal: May Your Majesty not make your servant plug the anomaly of surging water and meet Zheng Xian's death, to be laughed at by Yao Ping."
translations.idiomatic: Do not let me choke off the flood-omen only to share Zheng Xian's fate and become Yao Ping's jest."

sentences.zh: 房至陝，復上封事曰：「乃丙戌小雨，丁亥蒙氣去，然少陰並力而乘消息，戊子益甚，到五十分，蒙氣復起。

translations.literal: When Fang reached Shan he again submitted a sealed memorial, saying, "Then on bingxu there was light rain; on dinghai the shrouding vapor departed, yet lesser yin combined forces and rode the waxing and waning; on wuzi it grew worse, reaching fifty parts, and the shrouding vapor rose again.
translations.idiomatic: From Shan he sent a third sealed memorial: "On bingxu a light rain fell; on dinghai the haze lifted, yet minor yin massed and rode the cycle; by wuzi it was worse—at the fiftieth mark the murk returned.

sentences.zh: 此陛下欲正消息，雜卦之黨並力而爭，消息之氣不勝。

translations.literal: This is Your Majesty wishing to correct the waxing and waning; the faction of miscellaneous hexagrams combines its force and contends; the vapor of waxing and waning does not overcome.
translations.idiomatic: You mean to straighten the cosmic pulse, but the clique of 'mixed' hexagrams pushes back until the true rise-and-fall breath cannot win.

sentences.zh: 強弱安危之機不可不察。

translations.literal: The pivot of strength and weakness, safety and peril, must not go unexamined.
translations.idiomatic: The turning point between strength and weakness, safety and ruin, cannot be ignored.

sentences.zh: 己丑夜，有還風，盡辛卯，太陽復侵色，至癸巳，日月相薄，此邪陰同力而太陽為之疑也。

translations.literal: On the night of jichou there was a returning wind; through to xinmao the great yang again suffered encroachment on its color; when it reached guisi sun and moon pressed each other—this is evil yin acting in concert and the great yang made doubtful for it.
translations.idiomatic: That night of jichou a backing wind blew until xinmao; the sovereign sun dimmed again; by guisi sun and moon closed on each other—wicked yin has joined ranks and left the great yang uncertain.

sentences.zh: 臣前白九年不改，必有星亡之異。

translations.literal: Your servant previously reported that if nine years passed without reform there would surely be the anomaly of a star's extinction.
translations.idiomatic: I warned before: nine years without reform would bring the omen of a vanishing star.

sentences.zh: 臣願出任良試考功，臣得居內，星亡之異可去。

translations.literal: Your servant wishes to have Ren Liang try the merit examination abroad while your servant remains within—then the anomaly of the star's extinction may be removed.
translations.idiomatic: Let Ren Liang trial the merit law in the provinces while I stay at court—that omen can still be lifted.

sentences.zh: 議者知如此於身不利，臣不可蔽，故云使弟子不若試師。

translations.literal: Those who deliberated knew that thus it would be unprofitable to themselves; your servant could not be blocked; therefore they said sending disciples was not as good as trying the master.
translations.idiomatic: The critics knew that plan would hurt them; they could not silence me outright, so they said, 'Test the teacher, not the pupils.'

sentences.zh: 臣為刺史又當奏事，故復雲為刺史恐太守不與同心，不若以為太守，此其所以隔絕臣也。

translations.literal: If your servant were regional inspector he would again have to memorialize; therefore they again said that as regional inspector he would fear the grand governor would not be of one mind with him—not so good as making him grand governor: this is why they cut off your servant.
translations.idiomatic: If I were an inspector I could still memorialize—so they argued an inspector might feud with his governor; better make me the governor himself—that is how they walled me off.

sentences.zh: 陛下不違其言而遂聽之，此乃蒙氣所以不解，太陽亡色者也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty did not go against their words but followed them—this is why the shrouding vapor does not disperse and the great yang loses its color.
translations.idiomatic: You did not reject their advice but went along with it—hence the haze will not lift and the imperial sun has lost its color.

sentences.zh: 臣去朝稍遠，太陽侵色益甚，唯陛下毋難還臣而易逆天意。

translations.literal: The farther your servant departs from court, the more the great yang suffers encroachment on its color; may Your Majesty not find it hard to recall your servant and thus lightly go against Heaven's intent.
translations.idiomatic: The farther I am driven from the capital, the deeper the sun is eclipsed—do not think it too hard to call me back and so lightly defy Heaven.

sentences.zh: 邪說雖安於人，天氣必變，故人可欺，天不可欺也，願陛下察焉。」

translations.literal: Though heterodox doctrines may satisfy men, the weather of Heaven must change—therefore men may be deceived, Heaven may not be deceived; may Your Majesty examine this."
translations.idiomatic: False doctrine may comfort men for a season, but Heaven's temper will turn—men can be fooled, Heaven cannot. I beg you to weigh this."

sentences.zh: 房去月餘，竟征下獄。

translations.literal: A little more than a month after Fang departed, he was finally summoned and cast into prison.
translations.idiomatic: Within a month of his departure he was arrested and thrown into jail.

sentences.zh: 初，淮陽憲王舅張博從房受學，以女妻房。

translations.literal: Originally Zhang Bo, maternal uncle of Prince Xian of Huaiyang, studied under Fang and gave his daughter to Fang as wife.
translations.idiomatic: At the outset Zhang Bo—maternal uncle to Prince Xian of Huaiyang—had been Fang's student and married him a daughter.

sentences.zh: 房與相親，每朝見，輒為博道其語，以為上意欲用房議，而群臣惡其害己，故為眾所排。

translations.literal: Fang was intimate with him; whenever they met at court he always told Bo the sovereign's words, thinking the sovereign's mind wished to employ Fang's proposals, but the host of ministers hated the harm to themselves, therefore he was pushed aside by the multitude.
translations.idiomatic: They were close; at each court meeting Fang repeated the emperor's remarks, sure that the throne meant to use his reforms, while the ministers, fearing for their own positions, had closed ranks against him.

sentences.zh: 博曰：「淮陽王上親弟，敏達好政，欲為國忠。

translations.literal: Bo said, "The king of Huaiyang is the sovereign's own younger brother, keen and adept and fond of government; he wishes to be loyal to the state.
translations.idiomatic: Bo said, "The Prince of Huaiyang is your own brother—quick-witted and eager for good government, anxious to serve the realm.

sentences.zh: 今欲令王上書求入朝，得佐助房。」

translations.literal: Now we wish to have the king submit a memorial asking to enter court, so as to be able to assist Fang."
translations.idiomatic: Have him memorialize for permission to attend court—there he can lend you his voice."

sentences.zh: 房曰：「得無不可？」

translations.literal: Fang said, "Could there be anything improper in that?"
translations.idiomatic: Fang asked, "Is there any risk in that?"

sentences.zh: 博曰：「前楚王朝薦士，何為不可？」

translations.literal: Bo said, "Formerly the king of Chu entered court and recommended scholars—why should it not be permitted?"
translations.idiomatic: Bo answered, "The king of Chu once came to court to recommend talent—where is the harm?"

sentences.zh: 房曰：「中書令石顯、尚書令五鹿君相與合同，巧佞之人也，事縣官十餘年；

translations.literal: Fang said, "Palace secretary Shi Xian and secretary of the masters of writing Wulu Jun act in concert together; they are clever flatterers; they have served the imperial house more than ten years;
translations.idiomatic: Fang replied, "Palace Secretary Shi Xian and Secretary Wulu Jun move as one pair of sycophants; they have clung to the Han court for more than a decade;

sentences.zh: 及丞相韋侯，皆久亡補於民，可謂亡功矣。

translations.literal: together with Chancellor Wei Hou, all have long been without supplement to the people—one may call them without merit.
translations.idiomatic: and Chancellor Wei Xuan together—they have long done nothing to benefit the people. They are men without real achievement.

sentences.zh: 此尤不欲行考功者也。

translations.literal: These especially do not wish to carry out the merit examination.
translations.idiomatic: Above all they dread your merit system.

sentences.zh: 淮陽王即朝見，勸上行考功，事善；

translations.literal: If the king of Huaiyang attends court and urges the sovereign to carry out the merit examination, the affair will be good;
translations.idiomatic: If the prince reaches audience and urges the merit law, well and good;

sentences.zh: 不然，但言丞相、中書令任事久而不治，可休丞相，以御史大夫鄭弘代之，遷中書令置他官，以鉤盾令徐立代之，如此，房考功事得施行矣。」

translations.literal: if not, let him only say that the chancellor and the palace secretary have held office long yet govern badly—then the chancellor may be retired and imperial counsellor Zheng Hong replace him; move the palace secretary to another office and have hook-and-shield director Xu Li replace him—thus Fang's merit-examination affair can be carried out."
translations.idiomatic: if not, let him say plainly that chancellor and palace secretary have grown stale in power yet rule badly—dismiss the chancellor and elevate Imperial Counsellor Zheng Hong; transfer the palace secretary and put Hook-and-Shield Director Xu Li in his place—then my reforms can go forward."

sentences.zh: 博具從房記諸所說災異事，因令房為淮陽王作求朝奏草，皆持柬與淮陽王。

translations.literal: Bo fully followed Fang in recording all the matters of portents and anomalies that had been spoken; then he ordered Fang to draft for the king of Huaiyang a memorial seeking audience, and all carried letters to the king of Huaiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Bo wrote down every omen Fang had discussed, had Fang draft the prince's request to attend court, and carried the papers to Huaiyang.

sentences.zh: 石顯微司具知之，以房親近，未敢言。

translations.literal: Shi Xian's secret agents fully knew it; because Fang was intimate with the sovereign they did not dare speak.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Xian's informers learned every detail, but while Fang stayed near the emperor they held their tongues.

sentences.zh: 及房出守郡，顯告房與張博通謀，非謗政治，歸惡天子，詿誤諸侯王，語在《憲王傳》。

translations.literal: When Fang went out to guard the commandery, Xian reported that Fang and Zhang Bo had conspired together, slandered the government, returned evil to the Son of Heaven, and misled the feudal kings—the words are in the "Biography of Prince Xian."
translations.idiomatic: Once Fang left for his post, Xian denounced him and Zhang Bo for conspiracy, defaming the government, shifting blame onto the emperor, and misleading a feudal prince—the full account is in the biography of Prince Xian.

sentences.zh: 初，房見道幽、厲事，出為御史大夫鄭弘言之。

translations.literal: Originally when Fang spoke of the affairs of You and Li of Zhou on the road, going out he had told them to Imperial Counsellor Zheng Hong.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, when Fang had spoken of King You and King Li on the road, he had repeated the same to Imperial Counsellor Zheng Hong.

sentences.zh: 房、博皆棄市，弘坐免為庶人。

translations.literal: Fang and Bo were both exposed in the market; Hong by implication was stripped to commoner.
translations.idiomatic: Fang and Bo were executed in the marketplace; Zheng Hong was dismissed to commoner status for his connection.

sentences.zh: 房本姓李，推律自定為京氏，死時年四十一。

translations.literal: Fang's original surname was Li; by extending the pitch pipes he fixed himself as the Jing clan; at death his age was forty-one.
translations.idiomatic: Fang had been born a Li; working from the pitch-pipes he took the surname Jing. He was forty-one when he died.

sentences.zh: 時，平昌侯王臨以宣帝外屬侍中，稱詔欲從奉學其術。

translations.literal: At the time Marquis of Pingchang Wang Lin, as a maternal relative of Emperor Xuan, was palace attendant; citing an edict he wished to follow Feng and study his arts.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Marquis Wang Lin of Pingchang, a kinsman on Emperor Xuan's mother's side, served as palace attendant and, claiming imperial word, asked to study under Feng's methods.

sentences.zh: 奉不肯與言，而上封事曰：「臣聞之於師，治道要務，在知下之邪正。

translations.literal: Feng refused to speak with him and submitted a sealed memorial, saying, "Your servant has heard from his teacher: in the essentials of governing the Way, it lies in knowing whether those below are deviant or correct.
translations.idiomatic: Feng would not lecture him and sent up a sealed note instead: "My teacher taught me that the crux of rule is to know which subordinates are straight and which are crooked.

sentences.zh: 人誠鄉正，雖愚為用；

translations.literal: If men truly incline to the correct, though stupid they are of use;
translations.idiomatic: Men who lean toward integrity remain useful even if dull;

sentences.zh: 若乃懷邪，知益為害。

translations.literal: if they harbor deviance in the breast, knowledge only increases the harm.
translations.idiomatic: men who nurse treachery only grow more dangerous the cleverer they are.

sentences.zh: 知下之術，在於六情十二律而已。

translations.literal: The art of knowing those below lies in the six affections and twelve pitches—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: The way to read them is the six emotions set against the twelve pitch-pipes—nothing more arcane than that.

sentences.zh: 北方之情，好也；

translations.literal: The affection of the north is 'fondness';
translations.idiomatic: In the north the dominant mood is desire—'fondness';

sentences.zh: 好行貪狼，申子主之。

translations.literal: when fondness acts it runs to covetous greed; shen and zi govern it.
translations.idiomatic: when it moves it turns greedy and rapacious; the branches shen and zi govern it.

sentences.zh: 東方之情，怒也；

translations.literal: The affection of the east is 'anger';
translations.idiomatic: In the east the mood is anger;

sentences.zh: 怒行陰賊，亥卯主之。

translations.literal: when anger acts it runs to hidden injury; hai and mao govern it.
translations.idiomatic: when it stirs it becomes covert malice; hai and mao govern it.

sentences.zh: 貪狼必待陰賊而後動，陰賊必待貪狼而後用，二陰並行，是以王者忌子卯也。

translations.literal: Covetous greed must wait for hidden injury and then moves; hidden injury must wait for covetous greed and then is employed; the two yins move together—therefore kings taboo zi and mao.
translations.idiomatic: Rapacity waits on stealth to strike, and stealth needs rapacity to serve—two yin forces in harness; that is why the sage-kings feared the zi and mao days.

sentences.zh: 《禮經》避之，《春秋》諱焉。

translations.literal: The ritual classics avoid them; the Spring and Autumn Annals treat them as taboo.
translations.idiomatic: The ritual canon shuns them; the Spring and Autumn records them as unlucky.

sentences.zh: 南方之情，惡也；

translations.literal: The affection of the south is 'loathing';
translations.idiomatic: In the south the mood is loathing;

sentences.zh: 惡行廉貞，寅午主之。

translations.literal: when loathing acts it runs to incorrupt constancy; yin and wu govern it.
translations.idiomatic: when it moves it hardens into stern integrity; yin and wu govern it.

sentences.zh: 西方之情，喜也；

translations.literal: The affection of the west is 'joy';
translations.idiomatic: In the west the mood is joy;

sentences.zh: 喜行寬大，己酉主之。

translations.literal: when joy acts it runs to magnanimity; ji and you govern it.
translations.idiomatic: when it stirs it opens into generosity; ji and you govern it.

sentences.zh: 二陽並行，是以王者吉午酉也。

translations.literal: The two yangs move together—therefore kings treat wu and you as auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Two yang forces in concert—hence the wu and you days are reckoned fortunate for the throne.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：『吉日庚午。』

translations.literal: The Odes says, 'Auspicious is the day gengwu.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says, 'Lucky is the gengwu day.'

sentences.zh: 上方之情，樂也；

translations.literal: The affection of the upper direction is 'pleasure';
translations.idiomatic: Above, the mood is pleasure;

sentences.zh: 樂行奸邪，辰未主之。

translations.literal: when pleasure acts it runs to wickedness and deviance; chen and wei govern it.
translations.idiomatic: when it runs unchecked it slides into treachery; chen and wei govern it.

sentences.zh: 下方之情，哀也；

translations.literal: The affection of the lower direction is 'grief';
translations.idiomatic: Below, the mood is grief;

sentences.zh: 哀行公正，戌丑主之。

translations.literal: when grief acts it runs to upright fairness; xu and chou govern it.
translations.idiomatic: when it deepens it steadies into justice; xu and chou govern it.

sentences.zh: 辰未屬陰，戌丑屬陽，萬物各以其類應。

translations.literal: Chen and wei belong to yin; xu and chou belong to yang—each of the myriad things responds according to its kind.
translations.idiomatic: Chen and wei are yin branches, xu and chou are yang—creation answers each class in kind.

sentences.zh: 今陛下明聖虛靜以待物至，萬事雖眾，何聞而不諭，豈況乎執十二律而御六情！

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty is enlightened and sage, empty and still awaiting the arrival of things; though the myriad affairs are many, what have you heard and not understood—how much more if you hold the twelve pitches and govern the six affections!
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty is clear-sighted and still, waiting on events as they come; though affairs crowd in, what reaches your ear that you do not grasp—and how much more if you wield the twelve pitches to master the six emotions!

sentences.zh: 於以知下參實，亦甚優矣，萬不失一，自然之道也。

translations.literal: By this to know those below and verify substance is also very excellent; of ten thousand not one is lost—it is the way of spontaneity.
translations.idiomatic: From that you can test men below with near infallibility—ten thousand in ten thousand, the natural course of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 乃正月癸未日加申，有暴風從西南來。

translations.literal: Then on guiwei of the first month, at the double-hour shen, there was a violent wind from the southwest.
translations.idiomatic: On guiwei in the first month, at the shen hour, a gale blew from the southwest.

sentences.zh: 未主奸邪，申主貪狼，風以大陰下抵建前，是人主左右邪臣之氣也。

translations.literal: Wei governs wickedness and deviance; shen governs covetous greed; the wind with great yin below struck Jian forward—this is the vapor of evil ministers to the ruler's left and right.
translations.idiomatic: Wei stands for treachery, shen for rapacity; the wind drove great yin against the Jian position—the breath of corrupt ministers flanking the throne.

sentences.zh: 平昌侯比三來見臣，皆以正辰加邪時。

translations.literal: Marquis of Pingchang three times came to see your servant; each time it was with correct chen adding to a deviant double-hour.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Pingchang called on me three times, each visit falling when a true chen hour met a false time-branch.

sentences.zh: 辰為客，時為主人。

translations.literal: Chen is guest; the double-hour is host.
translations.idiomatic: In that reckoning chen is the guest, the hour the host.

sentences.zh: 以律知人情，王者之秘道也，愚臣誠不敢以語邪人。」

translations.literal: By the pitches to know human dispositions is the secret way of kings; your foolish servant truly dares not speak of it to deviant men."
translations.idiomatic: Reading human hearts through the pipes is the sovereign's hidden art—your humble servant dare not divulge it to the crooked."

sentences.zh: 上以奉為中郎，召問奉：「來者以善日邪時，孰與邪日善時？」

translations.literal: The sovereign made Feng a gentleman of the palace and summoned him to ask, "When the arrival is on a good day in a deviant double-hour, compared with a deviant day in a good double-hour—which is which?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor named Feng a gentleman of the palace and asked: "Which counts for more—a lucky day with an unlucky hour, or the reverse?"

sentences.zh: 奉對曰：「師法用辰不用日。

translations.literal: Feng replied, saying, "In master's method one uses chen, not the day.
translations.idiomatic: Feng answered, "Our school relies on the stem-branch chen, not on the calendar day.

sentences.zh: 辰為客，時為主人。

translations.literal: Chen is guest; the double-hour is host.
translations.idiomatic: Chen plays guest to the hour's host.

sentences.zh: 見於明主，侍者為主人。

translations.literal: When appearing before an enlightened sovereign, the attendant is host.
translations.idiomatic: In audience with a wise ruler, the attendant becomes the host.

sentences.zh: 辰正時邪，見者正，侍者邪；

translations.literal: When chen is correct and the double-hour deviant, the one who appears is correct, the attendant deviant;
translations.idiomatic: If chen is true and the hour false, the petitioner is straight and the attendant crooked;

sentences.zh: 辰邪時正，見者邪，侍者正。

translations.literal: when chen is deviant and the double-hour correct, the one who appears is deviant, the attendant correct.
translations.idiomatic: if chen is false and the hour true, the petitioner is crooked and the attendant straight.

sentences.zh: 忠正之見，侍者雖邪，辰時俱正；

translations.literal: In the appearance of loyalty and correctness, although the attendant is deviant, chen and the double-hour are both correct;
translations.idiomatic: When a loyal man comes forward, even a wicked attendant cannot spoil the hour if chen and the time align;

sentences.zh: 大邪之見，侍者雖正，辰時俱邪。

translations.literal: in the appearance of great deviance, although the attendant is correct, chen and the double-hour are both deviant.
translations.idiomatic: when a great villain presents himself, even an honest attendant cannot save the omen if chen and the hour both turn false.

sentences.zh: 即以自知侍者之邪，而時邪辰正，見者反邪；

translations.literal: From this one knows by oneself the attendant's deviance, yet the double-hour is deviant and chen correct, and the one who appears is contrary deviant;
translations.idiomatic: Thus you may detect a corrupt attendant yet find the hour false while chen stays true—the petitioner then seems false;

sentences.zh: 即以自知侍者之正，而時正辰邪，見者反正。

translations.literal: from this one knows by oneself the attendant's correctness, yet the double-hour is correct and chen deviant, and the one who appears is contrary correct.
translations.idiomatic: or you may trust an upright attendant while the hour is true and chen false—then the petitioner seems true when he is not.

sentences.zh: 辰為常事，時為一行。

translations.literal: Chen is constant affairs; the double-hour is one line.
translations.idiomatic: Chen governs the standing pattern; the hour marks a single line of change.

sentences.zh: 辰疏而時精，其效同功，必參五觀之，然後可知。

translations.literal: Chen is coarse and the double-hour refined; their effects share the same work—one must triangulate and examine five ways, then one can know.
translations.idiomatic: Chen is broad, the hour fine-grained; both must be weighed together—cross-check five ways before you judge.

sentences.zh: 故曰：察其所繇，省其進退，參之六合五行，則可以見人性，知人情。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: examine that from which things proceed, inspect their advance and retreat, triangulate with the six harmonies and the five phases—then one may see human nature and know human dispositions.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: trace every motive, watch advance and retreat, set the six coordinates and five phases against one another—then you will see nature and read the heart.

sentences.zh: 難用外察，從中甚明，故詩之為學，情性而已。

translations.literal: It is hard to use outer inspection; from within it is very clear—therefore the learning of the Odes is disposition and nature, that is all.
translations.idiomatic: Outward signs mislead; the inner pattern is plain—that is why the Odes are finally a study of temperament and inborn character.

sentences.zh: 五性不相害，六情更興廢。

translations.literal: The five natures do not harm one another; the six affections alternate in rise and fall.
translations.idiomatic: The five temperaments do not cancel one another; the six emotions wax and wane in turn.

sentences.zh: 觀性以歷，觀情以律，明主所宜獨用，難與二人共也。

translations.literal: Observe nature by the calendar; observe disposition by the pitches—what an enlightened sovereign ought to use alone, hard to share with two others.
translations.idiomatic: Read nature through the calendar, disposition through the pipes—an instrument fit for the sovereign alone, not to be shared with a partner.

sentences.zh: 故曰：『顯諸仁，臧諸用。』

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: 'It shows in benevolence, it hides in function.'
translations.idiomatic: As the classic says, 'It displays as humanity; it stores itself in use.'

sentences.zh: 露之則不神，獨行則自然矣，唯奉能用之，學者莫能行。」

translations.literal: If exposed it is not numinous; if practiced alone it is spontaneous—only Feng can use it; scholars cannot carry it out."
translations.idiomatic: Lay it bare and the mystery dies; keep it in solitary practice and nature takes its course—only Feng can wield it; other students cannot."

sentences.zh: 是歲，關東大水，郡國十一饑，疫尤甚。

translations.literal: That year east of the Pass there was great flood; eleven commanderies and kingdoms suffered famine, and pestilence was especially severe.
translations.idiomatic: That year the country east of the Pass drowned in flood; eleven commanderies starved, and plague struck hardest there.

sentences.zh: 上乃下詔江海陂湖園池屬少府者以假貧民，勿租稅；

translations.literal: The sovereign thereupon issued an edict that rivers, seas, dikes, lakes, gardens, and pools belonging to the privy treasurer be lent to the poor and destitute, without rent or tax;
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered marshes, lakes, and imperial ponds under the privy treasurer thrown open to the poor, rent-free;

sentences.zh: 損大官膳，減樂府員，損苑馬，諸官館稀御幸者勿繕治；

translations.literal: reduce the victuals of the grand provisioner, cut the personnel of the music bureau, reduce the park horses, and for official lodges rarely favored by imperial progress do not repair;
translations.idiomatic: cut the grand provisioner's kitchen budget, trim the music bureau's staff, reduce the stud herds, and leave untended any lodge the court rarely visited;

sentences.zh: 太僕、少府減食谷馬，水衡省食肉獸。

translations.literal: the grand coachman and privy treasurer reduce grain-fed horses; the superintendent of waters and parks cuts flesh-eating beasts.
translations.idiomatic: the grand coachman and privy treasurer cut grain for carriage horses; the waterworks office slashed meat for the palace menagerie.

sentences.zh: 明年二月戊午，地震。

translations.literal: In the second month of the next year, on wuwu, there was an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, on wuwu in the second month, the earth shook.

sentences.zh: 其夏，劉地人相食。

translations.literal: That summer in the lands of Liu men ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, in the old Liu heartland, people turned to cannibalism.

sentences.zh: 七月己酉，地復震。

translations.literal: In the seventh month on jiyou the earth again quaked.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month, day jiyou, the earth shook again.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「蓋聞賢聖在位，陰陽和，風雨時，日月光，星辰靜，黎庶康寧，考終厥命。

translations.literal: The sovereign said, "We have heard that when sages and worthies are on the throne, yin and yang are harmonious, wind and rain come in season, sun and moon shine, the stars are still, and the black-haired people are healthy and peaceful, completing their allotted spans."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said: "They say that when true kings hold power, yin and yang balance, the seasons keep time, the luminaries shine clear, the stars hold their stations, and the common folk live out their years in peace."

sentences.zh: 今朕共承天地，托於公侯之上，明不能燭，德不能綏，災異並臻，連年不息。

translations.literal: Now We together receive Heaven and Earth and stand entrusted above the dukes and marquises; Our enlightenment cannot illumine, Our virtue cannot pacify; portents and anomalies arrive together and year after year do not cease.
translations.idiomatic: Yet I have received Heaven and earth and rest on my nobles, and my light is too dim and my virtue too weak to quiet the realm—omens pile up year after year without end."

sentences.zh: 乃二月戊午，地大震於隴西郡，毀落太上皇廟殿壁木飾，壞敗道縣城郭官寺及民室屋，厭殺人眾，山崩地裂，水泉湧出。

translations.literal: Then on wuwu in the second month the great earth shook in Longxi commandery, smashing down the wall-wood ornaments of the hall of the temple to the August Grandfather, ruining the walls of Dao county, city walls, official monasteries, and commoners' houses, crushing and killing a multitude of people; mountains collapsed and the earth split, springs gushed forth.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu in the second month Longxi was hit by a violent quake: the shrine to the Grand Progenitor lost its carved panels, Dao county's walls and towers fell, and countless people were crushed as hills split and water burst from the ground."

sentences.zh: 一年地再動，天惟降災，震驚朕躬。

translations.literal: In one year the earth moved twice; Heaven has sent down disaster and shocked Our person.
translations.idiomatic: Twice in one year the ground has buckled—Heaven is punishing me personally."

sentences.zh: 治有大虧，咎至於此。

translations.literal: In governing there is great deficiency; blame has reached this point.
translations.idiomatic: The fault lies in grave failures of government."

sentences.zh: 夙夜兢兢，不通大變，深懷郁悼，未知其序。

translations.literal: Morning and night We are fearful and trembling, not comprehending the great changes; deeply We harbor gloom and grief, not yet knowing their sequence.
translations.idiomatic: I lie awake anxious, unable to fathom these great convulsions, heavy with grief and still searching for their meaning."

sentences.zh: 比年不登，元元因乏，不勝饑寒，以陷刑辟，朕甚閔焉，□怛於心。

translations.literal: Year after year the harvest does not ripen; the common folk are destitute and cannot bear hunger and cold, thereby falling into punishments and laws—We greatly pity them, [lacuna] distressed in Our heart.
translations.idiomatic: Harvests have failed again and again; the people are worn down, driven by cold and hunger into crime. My heart aches for them—the manuscript marks a damaged graph in the text."

sentences.zh: 已詔吏虛倉廩，開府臧，振救貧民，群司其茂思天地之戒，有可蠲除減省以便萬姓者，各條奏。

translations.literal: We have already ordered officials to empty the granaries, open the treasury storehouses, and relieve the poor and destitute; let the host of offices earnestly ponder Heaven and Earth's warnings—whatever may be remitted, cut, or reduced to benefit the myriad surnames, let each itemize and memorialize.
translations.idiomatic: I have ordered granaries opened and stores unlocked for famine relief. Every office must search its conscience for what can be cut or waived to ease the people—memorialize each item plainly."

sentences.zh: 悉意陳朕過失，靡有所諱。」

translations.literal: State Our faults to the full; let there be nothing concealed."
translations.idiomatic: Lay my errors before me; hide nothing."

sentences.zh: 因赦天下，舉直言極諫之士。

translations.literal: Thereupon he amnestied the realm and raised men of straight words and utmost remonstrance.
translations.idiomatic: He proclaimed a general amnesty and called for blunt counsel.

sentences.zh: 奉奏封事曰：

translations.literal: Feng submitted a sealed memorial, saying,
translations.idiomatic: Feng answered with a sealed memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞之於師曰，天地設位，懸日月，布星辰，分陰陽，定四時，列五行，以視聖人，名之曰道。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard from his teacher: Heaven and Earth set out their positions, suspended sun and moon, spread the stars, divided yin and yang, fixed the four seasons, arrayed the five phases, to display themselves to the sage—this is named the Way.
translations.idiomatic: My teacher taught: Heaven and earth fix their stations, hang sun and moon, scatter stars, sort yin from yang, set the seasons, line up the five agents—all to instruct the sage. That pattern is called the Way.

sentences.zh: 聖人見道，然後知王治之象，故畫州土，建君臣，立律歷，陳成敗，以視賢者，名之曰經。

translations.literal: When the sage sees the Way, then he knows the image of kingly rule; therefore he draws the regions of the earth, establishes lord and minister, sets up pitch pipes and calendars, arrays success and failure, to display to the worthy—this is named the classic.
translations.idiomatic: The sage reads that pattern and grasps how kings should rule—so he maps the realm, orders ruler and minister, fixes pitch and calendar, and lays out what succeeds and fails for worthy men to study. That corpus is called the classics.

sentences.zh: 賢者見經，然後知人道之務，則《詩》、《書》、《易》、《春秋》、《禮》、《樂》是也。

translations.literal: When the worthy sees the classics, then he knows the tasks of the human Way—the Odes, Documents, Changes, Spring and Autumn, Ritual, and Music are this.
translations.idiomatic: The worthy opens those books and learns what humanity must do: the Odes, Documents, Changes, Spring and Autumn, Rituals, and Music.

sentences.zh: 《易》有陰陽，《詩》有五際，《春秋》有災異，皆列終始，推得失，考天心，以言王道之安危。

translations.literal: The Changes has yin and yang; the Odes has the five junctures; the Spring and Autumn has portents and anomalies—all array beginning and end, push gain and loss, examine the mind of Heaven, thereby to speak of the safety and peril of the kingly Way.
translations.idiomatic: The Changes tracks yin and yang; the Odes marks the five cosmic hinges; the Spring and Autumn records omens—each traces beginnings and ends, weighs success against failure, and reads Heaven's mind so we may judge whether the kingly Way stands or falls.

sentences.zh: 至秦乃不說，傷之以法，是以大道不通，至於滅亡。

translations.literal: Down to Qin they would not discourse on this and wounded it with punishments—therefore the great Way was blocked and they reached extinction.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin rejected such teaching and tried to silence it with law—so the great Way was choked off and the dynasty perished.

sentences.zh: 今陛下明聖，深懷要道，燭臨萬方，布德流惠，靡有闕遺。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty is enlightened and sage, deeply cherishing the essential Way, shines upon the myriad regions, spreads virtue and pours out kindness, without omission or defect.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty is wise and holds the essential Way dear; your light reaches every quarter, your kindness flows out with nothing withheld.

sentences.zh: 罷省不急之用，振救困貧，賦醫藥，賜棺錢，恩澤甚厚。

translations.literal: You have abolished and cut what is not urgently needed, relieved the distressed and poor, assessed fees for medicine, granted coffin money—favor and bounty are very thick.
translations.idiomatic: You have slashed needless spending, fed the hungry, paid for physic and burial—your grace runs deep.

sentences.zh: 又舉直言，求過失，盛德純備，天下幸甚。

translations.literal: Moreover you have raised straight-speaking men and sought faults—pure virtue is complete in fullness; the realm is greatly fortunate.
translations.idiomatic: You have called for honest criticism and asked where you err—such completeness of virtue is the empire's good fortune.

sentences.zh: 臣奉竊學《齊詩》，聞五際之要《十月之交》篇，知日蝕、地震之效昭然可明，猶巢居知風，穴處知雨，亦不足多，適所習耳。

translations.literal: Your servant Feng has privately studied the Qi Odes and heard that the crux of the five junctures lies in the piece 'The Intercourse of the Tenth Month'—there the effects of solar eclipse and earthquake are bright and can be clarified; it is like nesting aloft knowing the wind or dwelling in a burrow knowing the rain—not worth much praise, only what one is used to.
translations.idiomatic: I have studied the Qi Odes and the lesson of the five hinges in 'The Tenth Month's Encounter'—there eclipses and quakes read as clearly as a bird in a nest foretells wind or a beast in its den foretells rain. That is no marvel, only habit of study.

sentences.zh: 臣聞人氣內逆，則感動天地；

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that when human vapor turns inward contrary, then it moves Heaven and Earth;
translations.idiomatic: I am told that when human breath turns inward against the norm, it stirs Heaven and earth;

sentences.zh: 天變見於星氣日蝕，地變見於奇物震動。

translations.literal: Heaven's changes appear in star-vapor and solar eclipse; Earth's changes appear in prodigies and shaking.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven shows it in stars and eclipses; earth in monsters and tremors.

sentences.zh: 所以然者，陽用其精，陰用其形，猶人之有五髒六體，五臟象天，六體象地。

translations.literal: The reason it is so is that yang employs its quintessence and yin employs its form—like man having five viscera and six corporal parts: the five viscera image Heaven, the six corporal parts image Earth.
translations.idiomatic: Yang works through spirit-substance, yin through solid form—as in the body: the five organs mirror heaven, the six limbs mirror earth.

sentences.zh: 故髒病則氣色發於面，體病則欠申動於貌。

translations.literal: Therefore when the viscera are ill, color and vapor issue on the face; when the corporal parts are ill, yawning and stretching stir in the appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Sickness in the inner organs shows on the complexion; sickness in the frame shows in restless gesture.

sentences.zh: 今年太陰建於甲戌，律以庚寅初用事，歷以甲午從春。

translations.literal: This year Grand Yin is established in jiaxu; the pitch pipes take gengyin as first in operation; the calendar takes jiawu to follow spring.
translations.idiomatic: This year the Grand Yin cycle sets on jiaxu; the pipes begin their rule at gengyin; the calendar's spring rides on jiawu.

sentences.zh: 歷中甲庚，歷得參陽，性中仁義，情得公正貞廉，百年之精歲也。

translations.literal: In the calendar jia and geng obtain triple yang; in nature benevolence and righteousness obtain; in disposition fairness, uprightness, and integrity obtain—it is the quintessence of a hundred years' year.
