translations.idiomatic: Jia and geng in the calendar align three yang forces; in human nature they match benevolence and right; in temperament they match fair and steadfast mind—this is the distilled year of a full century.

sentences.zh: 正以精歲，本首王位，日臨中時接律而地大震，其後連月久陰，雖有大令，猶不能復，陰氣盛矣。

translations.literal: Just in this quintessence year, the foundation first occupies the king's position; when the sun stood at the middle double-hour it met the pitch pipes and the great earth shook greatly; afterward for successive months there was long overcast—although there were great orders, still they could not restore it; yin vapor has grown strong.
translations.idiomatic: At the year's hinge, as the sovereign hour met the pipes, the land convulsed; then month on month the sky stayed dark—great edicts could not lift the gloom. Yin has swollen to excess.

sentences.zh: 古者朝廷必有同姓以明親親，必有異姓以明賢賢，此聖王之所以大通天下也。

translations.literal: In antiquity the court necessarily had men of the same surname to clarify loving kin, and necessarily had men of different surname to clarify honoring the worthy—this is how sage kings greatly penetrated the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient courts balanced royal kin against outside talent—kin to bind the family, strangers to bring wisdom—by that mix the sage-kings held all under heaven.

sentences.zh: 同姓親而易進，異姓疏而難通，故同姓一，異姓五，乃為平均。

translations.literal: Men of the same surname are close and easy to advance; men of different surname are distant and hard to communicate with—therefore one same surname to five different surnames is then even balance.
translations.idiomatic: Kin are near and quick to promote; outsiders are remote and hard to hear—so the old ratio was one royal name against five others to keep the scales level.

sentences.zh: 今左右亡同姓，獨以舅後之家為親，異姓之臣又疏。

translations.literal: Now at left and right there are none of the same surname; alone the households of the two empresses' maternal kin are treated as kin, while ministers of different surname are again distant.
translations.idiomatic: Today no Liu clansmen flank the throne; only the two maternal houses count as family, while unrelated ministers stand far off.

sentences.zh: 二後之黨滿朝，非特處位，勢尤奢僭過度，呂、霍、上官足以卜之，甚非愛人之道，又非後嗣之長策也。

translations.literal: The factions of the two empresses fill the court; not only do they hold place, their power is especially extravagant and presumptuous beyond measure—Lü, Huo, and Shangguan are enough to divine it; it is very much not the Way of loving others, nor a long plan for posterity.
translations.idiomatic: The two dowager cliques pack the court—not merely in office but swollen past all measure. Lü, Huo, and the Shangguans foretell the end—this is no way to love the people or secure the next reign.

sentences.zh: 陰氣之盛，不亦宜乎！

translations.literal: That yin vapor should be strong—is it not fitting!
translations.idiomatic: Small wonder yin has grown so strong!

sentences.zh: 臣又聞未央、建章、甘泉宮才人各以百數，皆不得天性。

translations.literal: Your servant further has heard that at Weiyang, Jianzhang, and Sweet Springs palaces the talents each number in the hundreds, all unable to obtain Heaven's nature.
translations.idiomatic: I hear that Weiyang, Jianzhang, and Ganquan each keep hundreds of palace ladies—none of them able to fulfill their natural lives.

sentences.zh: 若杜陵園，其已御見者，臣子不敢有言，雖然，太皇太后之事也。

translations.literal: As for the Duling park, those already favored by imperial progress—servants dare not speak; nevertheless it is the Grand Empress Dowager's affair.
translations.idiomatic: As for the tombs at Duling and those the late emperor favored, a subject should hold his tongue—still, that touches the Grand Empress Dowager's domain.

sentences.zh: 及諸侯王園，與其後宮，宜為設員，出其過制者，此損陰氣應天救邪之道也。

translations.literal: As for the parks of the feudal kings together with their rear palaces, it is fitting to set quotas for them and dismiss those who exceed regulation—this is the Way of diminishing yin vapor, answering Heaven, and saving deviance.
translations.idiomatic: Princes' gardens and harems need fixed limits; send home everyone above quota—that cuts yin excess, answers Heaven, and checks wantonness.

sentences.zh: 今異至不應，災將隨之。

translations.literal: Now anomalies arrive and are not answered; disaster will follow them.
translations.idiomatic: If these signs go unanswered, worse will follow.

sentences.zh: 其法大水，極陰生陽，反為大旱，甚則有火災，春秋宋伯□是矣。

translations.literal: Its pattern is great flood; extreme yin begets yang and turns into great drought; in the extreme there is fire disaster—in the Spring and Autumn the Song earl [lacuna] was thus.
translations.idiomatic: The sequence runs flood, then yin pushed to the limit flips to scorching drought, then to fire—as with the Song lord in the Spring and Autumn annals—the text here is damaged.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下財察。

translations.literal: May Your Majesty examine with discretion.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to weigh this carefully.

sentences.zh: 明年夏四月乙未，孝武園白鶴館災。

translations.literal: In the summer of the next year, on yiwei in the fourth month, the White Crane Lodge in Emperor Wu's mausoleum park burned.
translations.idiomatic: The next summer, yiwei of the fourth month, fire destroyed the White Crane Lodge at Emperor Wu's tomb park.

sentences.zh: 奉自以為中，上疏曰：「臣前上五際地震之效，曰極陰生陽，恐有火災。

translations.literal: Feng himself thought he had hit the mark and submitted a memorial, saying, "Your servant previously reported the effect of the five junctures and earthquake, saying extreme yin begets yang and I feared there would be fire disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Feng believed his omen had struck home and wrote: "I warned that when yin peaks it turns to yang and brings fire—"

sentences.zh: 不合明聽，未見省答，臣竊內不自信。

translations.literal: It did not accord with enlightened hearing and received no examination or reply—your servant privately lost confidence within.
translations.idiomatic: I heard nothing back from the throne and began to doubt myself—"

sentences.zh: 今白鶴館以四月乙未，時加於卯，月宿亢災，與前地震同法。

translations.literal: Now the White Crane Lodge, on yiwei in the fourth month at the double-hour added to mao, the moon lodged in Kang as disaster—it is the same pattern as the previous earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: The lodge burned on yiwei at the mao hour while the moon stood in Kang—the same configuration as the earlier quake—"

sentences.zh: 臣奉乃深知道之可信也。

translations.literal: Your servant Feng then deeply knew that the Way may be trusted.
translations.idiomatic: —which proves the Way can be trusted."

sentences.zh: 不勝拳拳，願復賜間，卒其終始。」

translations.literal: Unable to bear earnest loyalty, I beg again to be granted a space and to finish its beginning and end."
translations.idiomatic: I press my case again, begging a hearing so I may carry the argument to its conclusion."

sentences.zh: 上復延問以得失。

translations.literal: The sovereign again prolonged audience and asked him concerning gain and loss.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned him again and asked what should be kept or changed.

sentences.zh: 奉以為祭天地於雲陽汾陰，及諸寢廟不以親疏迭毀，皆煩費，違古制。

translations.literal: Feng held that sacrificing to Heaven and Earth at Yunyang and Fenyin, and the various resting temples not being rotated in destruction according to nearness or distance, were all vexatiously costly and violated ancient institutions.
translations.idiomatic: Feng argued that worship at Yunyang and Fenyin and the round of imperial shrines, without the classical rotation of distant versus near temples, wasted wealth and broke old rite.

sentences.zh: 又宮室苑囿，奢泰難供，以故民困國虛，亡累年之畜。

translations.literal: Moreover palace halls and hunting parks, extravagant and hard to supply, on that account made the people exhausted and the state empty, without the store of successive years.
translations.idiomatic: Palaces and parks had grown too rich to maintain, beggaring the people and draining the treasury of its grain reserve.

sentences.zh: 所繇來久，不改其本，難以末正，乃上疏曰：

translations.literal: What they came from is long-standing; if one does not change the root, it is hard to correct the branch tips—thereupon he submitted a memorial, saying:
translations.idiomatic: The evil is deep-rooted; trim the twigs without healing the trunk and nothing holds—so he sent up a memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞昔者盤庚改邑以興殷道，聖人美之。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that in former times Pan Geng changed the capital city to revive the Yin Way, and the sage praised it.
translations.idiomatic: I have read how Pan Geng moved the Shang capital to renew the dynasty, and the sages praised him for it.

sentences.zh: 竊聞漢德隆盛，在於孝文皇帝躬行節儉，外省徭役。

translations.literal: I have heard that Han's virtue rose to splendor in that Emperor Wen the Filial in person practiced frugality and cut corvée labor in the outer regions.
translations.idiomatic: Han's golden age was Emperor Wen, who lived plainly and lightened the people's labor.

sentences.zh: 其時未有甘泉、建章及上林中諸離宮館也。

translations.literal: At that time there were not yet Sweet Springs, Jianzhang, and the various detached palaces and lodges in the Shanglin Park.
translations.idiomatic: Then there were no Sweet Springs, Jianzhang, or the string of lodges in Shanglin.

sentences.zh: 未央宮又無高門、武台、麒麟、鳳皇、白虎、玉堂、金華之殿，獨有前殿、曲台、漸台、宣室、溫室、承明耳。

translations.literal: In Weiyang Palace there were also not the halls of High Gate, Martial Terrace, Unicorn, Phoenix, White Tiger, Jade Hall, and Golden Splendor—there were only the Front Hall, Curved Terrace, Gradual Terrace, Bright Chamber, Hothouse, and Chengming.
translations.idiomatic: Weiyang held only the front hall, the curved and gradual terraces, the Bright Chamber, the hothouse, and Chengming—none of the later towers that crowd the site today.

sentences.zh: 孝文欲作一台，度用百金，重民之財，廢而不為，其積土基，至今猶存，又下遺詔，不起山墳。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen wished to build one terrace; estimating the cost at a hundred catties of gold, he prized the people's wealth and abandoned it without doing it; the piled earth foundation to this day still exists; moreover he left a testamentary edict not to raise a mountain tomb.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen once planned a terrace, priced it at a hundred catties of gold, and dropped the project rather than tax the people—the earthen base is still visible—and his death edict forbade a high tumulus.

sentences.zh: 故其時天下大和，百姓洽足，德流後嗣。

translations.literal: Therefore at that time the realm was greatly harmonious, the hundred surnames were amply provided, and virtue flowed to later heirs.
translations.idiomatic: Under that rule the empire knew great peace, the people were full-fed, and virtue passed to his heirs.

sentences.zh: 如令處於當今，因此制度，必不能成功名。

translations.literal: If one were placed in the present day, by these institutions one could certainly not accomplish fame and merit.
translations.idiomatic: Those institutions could not sustain the empire as it stands now.

sentences.zh: 天道有常，王道亡常，亡常者所以應有常也。

translations.literal: The Way of Heaven has constancy; the kingly Way has no constancy—what has no constancy is that whereby one answers what has constancy.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's pattern is fixed; the king's method must shift—the shifting answers what never moves.

sentences.zh: 必有非常之主，然後能立非常之功。

translations.literal: There must be an extraordinary sovereign, and only then can one establish extraordinary merit.
translations.idiomatic: An extraordinary age needs an extraordinary ruler before extraordinary deeds can be done.

sentences.zh: 臣願陛下徙都於成周，左據成皋，右阻黽池，前鄉崧高，後介大河，建滎陽，扶河東，南北千里以為關，而入敖倉；

translations.literal: Your servant begs Your Majesty to move the capital to Completed Zhou: on the left seize Chenggao, on the right block Meng Pool, forward face Mount Songgao, behind be bounded by the great river, establish Xingyang, support east of the river, take a thousand li south and north as the passes, and enter Aocang.
translations.idiomatic: Move the seat to the old Zhou capital: Chenggao on your left, the Meng ford on your right, Mount Song before you, the Yellow River at your back, Xingyang as your shield, a thousand li of rampart north and south, and the granaries of Ao within reach.

sentences.zh: 地方百里者八九，足以自娛；

translations.literal: Land of a hundred li eight or nine times is enough to amuse oneself.
translations.idiomatic: Eight or nine domains of a hundred li each would suffice for the court's needs.

sentences.zh: 東厭諸侯之權，西遠羌胡之難，陛下共已亡為，按成周之居，兼盤庚之德，萬歲之後，長為高宗。

translations.literal: East you press down the power of the feudal lords; west you distance the troubles of Qiang and Hu; Your Majesty in shared self-restraint practices non-action, presses the dwelling of Completed Zhou, joins the virtue of Pan Geng—after ten thousand years you will long be Gaozong.
translations.idiomatic: You would pin the eastern princes in place, push the Qiang and Hu troubles far to the west, rule from the Zhou plain in quiet like Pan Geng, and leave a name to rival Gaozong of Shang.

sentences.zh: 漢家郊兆寢廟祭祀之禮多不應古，臣奉誠難但居而改作，故願陛下遷都正本。

translations.literal: The Han family's suburban sites, resting temples, and sacrificial rites for the most part do not accord with antiquity; your servant Feng truly finds it hard merely to dwell and patch them—therefore I beg Your Majesty to move the capital and correct the root.
translations.idiomatic: Han suburban rites and shrines no longer match the classics; patching them in place is hopeless—I urge removal of the capital to set the root straight.

sentences.zh: 眾制皆定，亡復繕治宮館不急之費，歲可余一年之畜。

translations.literal: When the multitude of institutions are all fixed, there will be no more repairing palace lodges for non-urgent expense, and year by year there may remain a year's store.
translations.idiomatic: Once the new order is fixed, stop pouring silver into needless lodges—you can bank a full year's surplus every year.

sentences.zh: 臣聞三代之祖積德以王，然皆不過數百年而絕。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the ancestors of the Three Dynasties accumulated virtue and thereby became kings, yet none lasted more than several hundred years and then was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: The founders of the three ancient dynasties piled up virtue to win the throne, yet none lasted more than a few centuries.

sentences.zh: 周至成王，有上賢之材，因文、武之業，以周、召為輔，有司各敬其事，在位莫非其人。

translations.literal: When Zhou reached King Cheng, there was material of supreme worthiness; relying on the enterprises of Wen and Wu, with the dukes of Zhou and Shao as assistants, each office reverently handled its affairs, and none in place was not the right man.
translations.idiomatic: At King Cheng of Zhou the throne had a prodigy of a boy king, the legacy of Wen and Wu, the dukes of Zhou and Shao at his side, and every office filled by the right man.

sentences.zh: 天下甫二世耳，然周公猶作詩、書深戒成王，以恐失天下。

translations.literal: The realm had only gone through two generations, yet still the Duke of Zhou made the Documents and Odes to warn Cheng deeply, fearing to lose the realm.
translations.idiomatic: The realm had seen only two reigns, yet the Duke of Zhou still wrote Documents and Odes to frighten the young king with the cost of losing all.

sentences.zh: 《書》則曰：「王毋若殷王紂。」

translations.literal: The Documents then says, "O king, do not be like the Yin king Zhou."
translations.idiomatic: The Documents warns: "Do not become another King Zhou of Shang."

sentences.zh: 其《詩》則曰：「殷之未喪師，克配上帝；

translations.literal: Its Odes then says, "When Yin had not yet lost its hosts, it could match High God;
translations.idiomatic: The Odes add: "While Shang still held its armies, it could stand before High God;

sentences.zh: 宜監於殿，駿命不易。」

translations.literal: It is fitting to take warning from Yin—the graph in the text reads 殿, commonly emended to 殷; the great mandate is not easy."
translations.idiomatic: Take the fall of Yin as your mirror—the great mandate is not easily kept."

sentences.zh: 今漢初取天下，起於豐沛，以兵征伐，德化未洽，後世奢侈，國家之費當數代之用，非直費財，又乃費士。

translations.literal: Now Han at first took the realm, rising from Feng and Pei, by arms campaigning and attacking; moral transformation was not yet pervasive; later generations were extravagant, and the state's outlays equaled what several generations should use—not only wasting wealth, but also wasting men.
translations.idiomatic: Han rose from Feng and Pei by the sword before virtue had spread; later reigns spent what several generations should have hoarded—wasting not only gold but men.

sentences.zh: 孝武之世，暴骨四夷，不可勝數。

translations.literal: In the age of Emperor Wu the Martial, bones were exposed in the four barbarians, beyond counting.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu left bones whitening among the four barbarians beyond number.

sentences.zh: 有天下雖未久，至於陛下八世九主矣，雖有成王之明，然亡周、召之佐。

translations.literal: Although possessing the realm has not been long, down to Your Majesty there have been eight generations and nine lords; although there is the enlightenment of King Cheng, yet there are no assistants like the dukes of Zhou and Shao.
translations.idiomatic: We have had nine rulers in eight generations since the founding; you may match King Cheng in wit, but you lack Zhou and Shao at your elbow.

sentences.zh: 今東方連年饑饉，加之以疾疫，百姓菜色，或至相食。

translations.literal: Now the east has suffered famine year after year, with pestilence added; the hundred surnames show the color of greens, some even to eating one another.
translations.idiomatic: The east starves year on year, plague on top of famine; faces are the gray-green of hunger, and some have turned to cannibalism.

sentences.zh: 地比震動，天氣混濁，日光侵奪。

translations.literal: The earth repeatedly quakes; the weather of Heaven is turbid; sunlight suffers encroachment and seizure.
translations.idiomatic: The ground keeps shaking, the sky stays foul, and daylight is eaten away.

sentences.zh: 繇此言之，執國政者豈可以不懷怵惕而戒萬分之一乎！

translations.literal: Speaking from this, how can those who hold the state's government fail to harbor fear and trembling and guard against the one in ten thousand!
translations.idiomatic: If that does not terrify whoever holds the reins of state, nothing will.

sentences.zh: 故臣願陛下因天變而徙都，所謂與天下更始者也。

translations.literal: Therefore your servant begs Your Majesty, by Heaven's changes, to move the capital—this is what is called making a fresh start with the realm.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you: read these omens as Heaven's order to move the capital and give the empire a new beginning.

sentences.zh: 天道終而復始，窮則反本，故能延長而亡窮也。

translations.literal: The Way of Heaven ends and then begins again; when exhausted it returns to the root—therefore it can prolong extension and have no exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's cycle runs to its end and starts anew; spent force returns to the root—only so can the mandate renew without end.

sentences.zh: 今漢道未終，陛下本而始之，於以永世延祚，不亦優乎！

translations.literal: Now the Han Way is not yet ended; Your Majesty takes the root and begins it—would it not be excellent thereby to extend the age and lengthen the blessing!
translations.idiomatic: The Han mandate is not yet spent; you can reopen its spring at the source and win ages of blessing.

sentences.zh: 如因丙子之孟夏，順太陰以東行，到後七年之明歲，必有五年之餘蓄，然後大行考室之禮，雖周之隆盛，亡以加此。

translations.literal: If you follow the first month of summer in bingzi, comply with Grand Yin and march east, until the bright year after seven years hence, there will necessarily be more than five years' surplus store, and then you may greatly carry out the rite of examining the chamber—though Zhou's splendor, nothing could add to this.
translations.idiomatic: Ride the bingzi early summer, march east with Grand Yin, and seven years from now you will have five years' grain in store—then you may hold the "examining the chamber" rite in a splendor even Zhou never matched.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下留神，詳察萬世之策。

translations.literal: May Your Majesty fix your spirit and examine in detail the plan for ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: Fix your mind on it, sire, and weigh a policy for endless generations.

sentences.zh: 書奏，天子異其意，答曰：「問奉：今園廟有七，雲東徙，狀何如？」

translations.literal: When the writing was presented, the Son of Heaven thought his meaning strange and replied, saying, "We ask Feng: now the mausoleum temples are seven; you speak of moving east—what is the situation?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor found the proposal startling and wrote back: "You speak of seven imperial shrines and an eastern move—explain what you mean."

sentences.zh: 奉對曰「昔成王徙洛，般庚遷殷，其所避就，皆陛下所明知也。

translations.literal: Feng replied, saying, "In former times when King Cheng moved to Luo and Pan Geng crossed to Yin, what they avoided and what they approached—Your Majesty clearly knows them all.
translations.idiomatic: Feng answered: "King Cheng's shift to Luo and Pan Geng's crossing to Yin—you know what they fled and what they sought."

sentences.zh: 非有聖明，不能一變天下之道。

translations.literal: Without sage enlightenment, one cannot in one change unify the Way of the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Without a sage's clarity no single reform can reset the Way of the world."

sentences.zh: 臣奉愚戇狂惑，唯陛下裁赦。」

translations.literal: Your servant Feng is foolish, blunt, mad, and deluded—may Your Majesty judge and pardon."
translations.idiomatic: I have spoken rashly; I await your judgment and mercy."

sentences.zh: 其後，貢禹亦言當定迭毀禮，上遂從之。

translations.literal: Afterward Gong Yu also spoke that it was fitting to fix the rotating-destruction rites for temples, and the sovereign thereupon followed it.
translations.idiomatic: Later Gong Yu urged fixing the classical rotation of distant temples, and the emperor adopted it.

sentences.zh: 及匡衡為丞相，奏徙南北郊，其議皆自奉發之。

translations.literal: When Kuang Heng became chancellor, he memorialized to move the southern and northern suburban sacrifices; the proposals all had their origin in what Feng raised.
translations.idiomatic: When Kuang Heng took the chancellorship, his memorial to shift the suburban altars grew from seeds Feng had planted.

sentences.zh: 奉以中郎為博士、諫大夫，年老以壽終。

translations.literal: Feng from gentleman of the palace became erudite and grandee remonstrant; in old age he ended his life by the full span of years.
translations.idiomatic: Feng rose from gentleman of the palace to erudite and grandee remonstrant, and died full of years.

sentences.zh: 子及孫，皆以學在儒官。

translations.literal: Sons and grandsons all by learning held posts among the Confucian officials.
translations.idiomatic: His sons and grandsons all won office as Confucian scholars.

sentences.zh: 李尋字子長，平陵人也。

translations.literal: Li Xun, courtesy name Zichang, was a man of Pingling.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xun, style Zichang, came from Pingling.

sentences.zh: 治《尚書》，與張孺、鄭寬中同師。

translations.literal: He studied the Documents, together with Zhang Ru and Zheng Kuanzhong having the same teacher.
translations.idiomatic: He studied the Documents under the same master as Zhang Ru and Zheng Kuanzhong.

sentences.zh: 寬中等守師法教授，尋獨好《洪範》災異，又學天文月令陰陽。

translations.literal: Kuanzhong and the rest kept the master's method in teaching; Xun alone loved the Hong fan and portents and anomalies, and also studied astronomy, monthly ordinances, yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng and the others taught the orthodox line; Xun alone pursued the "Great Plan," omens, astronomy, monthly commands, and yin-yang lore.

sentences.zh: 事丞相翟方進，方進亦善為星曆，除尋為吏，數為翟侯言事。

translations.literal: He served Chancellor Zhai Fangjin; Fangjin was also good at star calendars and removed Xun as a clerk, repeatedly speaking affairs for Marquis Zhai.
translations.idiomatic: He entered the service of Chancellor Zhai Fangjin, who was skilled in star lore and took him on as a clerk; Xun often briefed the marquis on policy.

sentences.zh: 帝舅曲陽侯王根為大司馬票騎將軍，厚遇尋。

translations.literal: The emperor's maternal uncle Marquis of Quyang Wang Gen was grand marshal and general of agile cavalry and treated Xun generously.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's uncle, Marquis Wang Gen of Quyang, served as grand marshal and general of agile cavalry and favored Xun.

sentences.zh: 是時多災異，根輔政，數虛己問尋。

translations.literal: At this time there were many portents and anomalies; Gen assisted government and repeatedly emptied himself to ask Xun.
translations.idiomatic: Omens multiplied while Gen held power; he humbled himself again and again to ask Xun's reading.

sentences.zh: 尋見漢家有中衰厄會之象，其意以為且有洪水為災，乃說根曰：

translations.literal: Xun saw that the Han house bore the image of middle decline and fated crisis; his idea was that there would be flood disaster—thereupon he spoke to Gen, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Xun saw in the Han stars the shape of mid-dynasty decline and believed a great flood was coming—so he warned Gen:

sentences.zh: 《書》云「天聰明」，蓋言紫宮極樞，通位帝紀，太微四門，廣開大道，五經六緯，尊術顯士，翼張舒布，燭臨四海，少微處士，為比為輔，故次帝廷，女宮在後。

translations.literal: The Documents says "Heaven hears and sees"—this speaks of the Purple Palace and polar pivot communicating the emperor's reckoning, the four gates of Grand Tenuity opening the great road, the five classics and six wefts honoring arts and displaying scholars, spreading their wings to illumine the four seas; Lesser Tenuity for retired gentlemen serves as comparison and assistance, therefore it ranks next to the imperial court, and the ladies' palaces are behind.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents says Heaven hears and sees—meaning the pole star and Purple Palace chart the sovereign's fate, the four gates of Grand Tenuity open the great thoroughfare, the five canons and six wefts exalt learning, wings of stars spread light over the seas, Lesser Tenuity marks recluses who aid the throne, and the harem ranks behind the court of ministers.

sentences.zh: 聖人承天，賢賢易色，取法於此。

translations.literal: Sages receive Heaven; honoring the worthy they change color—taking model from this.
translations.idiomatic: The sage takes his cue from Heaven, prizing worthies over mere beauty—just as the sky ranks stars and consorts.

sentences.zh: 天官上相上將，皆顓面正朝，憂責甚重，要在得人。

translations.literal: The celestial officers of senior minister and senior general all turn their faces to the correct court; the burden of care is very heavy—the essential lies in obtaining the right men.
translations.idiomatic: The stars of chief minister and chief general face the throne squarely; their office is heavy—everything turns on choosing the right men.

sentences.zh: 得人之效，成敗之機，不可不勉也。

translations.literal: The effect of obtaining the right men, the pivot of success and failure—one must not fail to exert oneself therein.
translations.idiomatic: Success and failure hinge on those appointments; nothing deserves your effort more.

sentences.zh: 昔秦穆公說諓□之言，任仡仡之勇，身受大辱，社稷幾亡。

translations.literal: In former times Duke Mu of Qin delighted in [lacuna] fawning words and employed the bravery of the towering and fierce; his person received great shame and the altars of soil and grain nearly perished.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Mu of Qin once trusted slick flattery and reckoned on sheer bravado—he was humiliated on the field and nearly lost his state.

sentences.zh: 悔過自責，思惟黃髮，任用百里奚，卒伯西域，德列王道。

translations.literal: He repented and blamed himself, thought on gray hair, employed Baili Xi, and in the end became hegemon in the western regions, his virtue ranking with the kingly Way.
translations.idiomatic: He turned, blamed himself, listened to old men's counsel, raised Baili Xi, and ended as lord protector of the west—virtue fit for a true king.

sentences.zh: 二者禍福如此，可不慎哉！

translations.literal: Of these two, disaster and blessing were thus—can one fail to be cautious!
translations.idiomatic: Two paths—ruin and recovery. Which lesson will you take?

sentences.zh: 夫士者，國家之大寶，功名之本也。

translations.literal: The gentleman is the great treasure of the state and the root of fame and merit.
translations.idiomatic: Men of worth are the state's chief treasure and the foundation of lasting name.

sentences.zh: 將軍一門九候，二十硃輪，漢興以來，臣子貴盛，未嘗至此。

translations.literal: In your general's one gate there are nine marquises and twenty vermillion wheels; since Han arose, in the honor and splendor of ministers and sons there has never been a case reaching this.
translations.idiomatic: Nine marquises and twenty scarlet-wheeled chariots in a single clan—since Han began no ministerial house has climbed so high.

sentences.zh: 夫物盛必衰，自然之理，唯有賢友強輔，庶幾可以保身命，全子孫，安國家。

translations.literal: When a thing reaches splendor it must decline—this is the pattern of nature; only if there are worthy friends and strong assistants can one perhaps preserve body and life, complete sons and grandsons, and secure state and altars.
translations.idiomatic: Fullness invites reversal—that is nature's law. Worthy friends and firm allies alone can save your life, your line, and the realm.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「歷象日月星辰」，此言仰視天文，俯察地理，觀日月消息，侯星辰行伍，揆山川變動，參人民謠俗，以製法度，考禍福。

translations.literal: The Documents says, 'Calculate and image the sun, moon, and stars'—this speaks of looking up to observe Heaven's patterns, looking down to inspect earth's lines, watching the waxing and waning of sun and moon, observing the ranks of the stars' motions, measuring the changes of mountains and rivers, triangulating with the people's songs and customs, thereby to set laws and measures and examine disaster and blessing.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents tells kings to chart sun, moon, and stars—that means read the sky, study the land, track lunar phases and stellar courses, note how mountains and rivers shift, listen to folk songs, and from all that frame law and foresee fortune or ruin.

sentences.zh: 舉措悖逆，咎敗將至，征兆為之先見。

translations.literal: When conduct and measures contradict and oppose, blame and defeat will arrive; the portents and signs for it appear first.
translations.idiomatic: When policy runs against the norm, defeat follows—and Heaven sends warnings first.

sentences.zh: 明君恐懼修正，側身博問，轉禍為福；

translations.literal: The enlightened ruler in fear corrects himself, turns his body aside and broadly questions, and turns disaster into blessing;
translations.idiomatic: A wise king takes alarm, mends his ways, seeks wide counsel, and can turn ill omens to good;

sentences.zh: 不可救者，即蓄備以待之，故社稷亡憂。

translations.literal: what cannot be saved, then store provisions and await it—therefore the altars of soil and grain have no worry.
translations.idiomatic: what cannot be mended he meets with stored grain and ready arms—so the altars stay unshaken.

sentences.zh: 竊見往者赤黃四塞，地氣大發，動土竭民，天下擾亂之征也。

translations.literal: I have privately seen that in former times red and yellow filled the four quarters, earth's vapor greatly issued, moving earth and exhausting the people—the sign of disturbance and chaos in the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Lately the sky ran red and yellow, earth-breath boiled up, corvée stripped the people—that is the sign of a realm in turmoil.

sentences.zh: 彗星爭明，庶雄為桀，大寇之引也。

translations.literal: Comets vied in brightness; many heroes acted as Jie—the introduction of great banditry.
translations.idiomatic: Comets flared like rival suns; bold men played tyrant—the prelude to great rebellion.

sentences.zh: 此二者已頗效矣。

translations.literal: These two have already partly verified themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Both omens have already begun to prove true.

sentences.zh: 城中訛言大水，奔走上城，朝廷驚駭，女孽入宮，此獨未效。

translations.literal: In the capital false rumor spoke of great flood; people ran in panic onto the walls; the court was alarmed and terrified; female portents entered the palace—this alone has not yet verified.
translations.idiomatic: The capital buzzed with false tales of flood; crowds fled to the ramparts; the court panicked; a woman's omen entered the harem—that part of the sign has not yet played out.

sentences.zh: 間者重以水泉湧溢，旁宮闕仍出。

translations.literal: Recently moreover there has been repeated welling and overflow of water springs, appearing again beside palace gates.
translations.idiomatic: Since then springs have burst and flooded again and again at the palace gates.

sentences.zh: 月、太白入東井，犯積水，缺天淵。

translations.literal: Moon and Great White entered the Eastern Well, encroaching on Stored Water, breaking Heaven's Deep.
translations.idiomatic: Moon and Venus entered the Eastern Well, struck the "stored water" stars, and broke the "heavenly abyss" formation.

sentences.zh: 日數湛於極陽之色。

translations.literal: The sun repeatedly sank in the color of extreme yang.
translations.idiomatic: The sun again and again drowned in a glare of excess yang.

sentences.zh: 羽氣乘宮，起風積雲。

translations.literal: Feather-vapor rode the palace; wind arose and clouds piled.
translations.idiomatic: Mist like feathers veiled the palace; wind rose and clouds stacked.

sentences.zh: 又錯以山崩地動，河不用其道。

translations.literal: Moreover there were wrongly added mountain collapse and earth movement, the Yellow River not keeping its channel.
translations.idiomatic: Add to that landslides, quakes, and the Yellow River abandoning its bed.

sentences.zh: 盛冬雷電，潛龍為孽。

translations.literal: In deep winter thunder and lightning; lurking dragons became portents.
translations.idiomatic: Thunder rolled in deep winter—hidden dragons stirring ill.

sentences.zh: 繼以隕星流彗，維、填上見，日蝕有背鄉。

translations.literal: Continuing with falling stars and streaming comets, Jupiter and Saturn appeared above; solar eclipses had backs turned.
translations.idiomatic: Then meteors and comets; Jupiter and Saturn climbed the sky; eclipses showed a dark bite on the edge.

sentences.zh: 此亦高下易居，洪水之征也。

translations.literal: This too is the sign of high and low exchanging dwelling and great flood.
translations.idiomatic: These too mean heaven and earth trading places—the omen of deluge.

sentences.zh: 不憂不改，洪水乃欲蕩滌，流彗乃欲掃除；

translations.literal: If one does not worry and does not reform, the great flood will wish to wash and scour, the streaming comet will wish to sweep clean;
translations.idiomatic: Ignore them and the flood will scour the realm, the comet sweep it clean;

sentences.zh: 改之，則有年亡期。

translations.literal: if one reforms, then there will be years without end.
translations.idiomatic: heed them and you win harvests without end.

sentences.zh: 故屬者頗有變改，小貶邪猾，日月光精，時雨氣應，此皇天右漢亡已也，何況致大改之！

translations.literal: Therefore recently there have been considerable changes and reforms; petty wickedness was slightly demoted; sun and moon's light grew pure; seasonal rains and vapors responded—this is august Heaven aiding Han without cease; how much more if you bring a great reform!
translations.idiomatic: Lately small reforms have already thinned the court of sharpers; sun and moon shine clearer; rains come on time—Heaven still favors Han. Think what a great reform could do!

sentences.zh: 宜急博求幽隱，拔擢天士，任以大職。

translations.literal: It is fitting urgently and broadly to seek the hidden and reclusive, raise up and select men of Heaven's appointment, and entrust them with great office.
translations.idiomatic: Cast the net for hidden talent, raise heaven-sent men, give them weighty posts—at once.

sentences.zh: 諸□茸佞諂，抱虛求進，乃用殘賊酷虐聞者，若此之徒，皆嫉善憎忠，壞天文，敗地理，湧躍邪陰，湛溺太陽，為主結怨於民，宜以時廢退，不當得居位。

translations.literal: As for the various [lacuna] weak flatterers who embrace emptiness seeking advancement, and those employed who are cruel and violent to the hearing—such types all hate the good and detest the loyal, ruin Heaven's patterns and defeat earth's lines, leap in evil yin and drown the great sun, knotting resentment for the ruler among the people—they should in due course be dismissed and retired and ought not to be allowed to remain in place.
translations.idiomatic: Sweep out the hollow sycophants and the cruel favorites—the text marks a damaged graph—who warp Heaven's chart, wreck the land's harmony, swell yin and dim the sovereign sun, and store up popular hate. Strip them of office.

sentences.zh: 誠必行之，凶災銷滅，子孫之福不旋日而至。

translations.literal: If this is truly put into practice, fierce disaster will melt away, and the blessing of sons and grandsons will arrive without the sun's turning.
translations.idiomatic: Do it, and calamity dissolves; your heirs' fortune returns before the day is out.

sentences.zh: 政治感陰陽，猶鐵炭之低卬，見效可信者也。

translations.literal: Government and teaching move yin and yang like iron and charcoal rising and falling—the effect can be trusted.
translations.idiomatic: Policy steers yin and yang the way iron and charcoal answer heat—reliably, visibly.

sentences.zh: 及諸蓄水連泉，務通利之。

translations.literal: As for the various stored waters and linked springs, strive to open and profit them.
translations.idiomatic: Dig out blocked pools and linked springs; keep the waters moving.

sentences.zh: 修舊堤防，省池澤稅，以助損邪陰之盛。

translations.literal: Repair old dikes and embankments, reduce taxes on pools and marshes, thereby to help diminish the excess of evil yin.
translations.idiomatic: Mend old levees, cut taxes on ponds—another way to bleed off excess yin.

sentences.zh: 案行事，考變易，訛言之效，未嘗不至。

translations.literal: Investigate past conduct, examine changes and alterations—the effect of false rumors has never failed to arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Track how rumor has always foretold change—it never misses.

sentences.zh: 請征韓放，掾周敞、王望可與圖之。

translations.literal: I beg to summon Han Fang, and clerks Zhou Chang and Wang Wang—they can plan this with us.
translations.idiomatic: Summon Han Fang; aides Zhou Chang and Wang Wang can help draft the plan.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「縣象著明，莫大乎日月。」

translations.literal: The Changes says, 'Suspended images most clear and bright—nothing greater than sun and moon.'
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says the clearest emblems hung in the sky are sun and moon.

sentences.zh: 夫日者，眾陽之長，輝光所燭，萬里同晷，人君之表也。

translations.literal: The sun is the chief of the many yangs; where its radiance shines, ten thousand li share one gnomon shadow—it is the model of the lord of men.
translations.idiomatic: The sun heads all yang; under one noon shadow the realm is one—emblem of the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 故日將旦，清風發，群陰伏，君以臨朝，不牽於色。

translations.literal: Therefore when the sun is about to dawn, clear wind arises and the host of yin hides; the ruler thereby faces court, not dragged by sensual color.
translations.idiomatic: At false dawn a clean wind rises and yin flees—the king should face his ministers, not linger in the harem.

sentences.zh: 日初出，炎以陽，君登朝，佞不行，忠直進，不蔽障。

translations.literal: When the sun first emerges, blazing with yang, the ruler ascends court; flattery does not advance, the loyal and straight come forward, not blocked and screened.
translations.idiomatic: At sunrise yang flares—the king holds court; sycophants hold back, honest men speak.

sentences.zh: 日中輝光，君德盛明，大臣奉公。

translations.literal: At noon splendor and radiance, the ruler's virtue is abundant and bright, great ministers uphold the public good.
translations.idiomatic: Noon: full light, the king's virtue at flood, ministers serve the common weal.

sentences.zh: 日將入，專以一，君就房，有常節。

translations.literal: When the sun is about to enter, it specializes in unity; the ruler retires to the inner chambers, with constant measure.
translations.idiomatic: Sunset: one disk alone—the king withdraws to the inner rooms on a steady schedule.

sentences.zh: 君不修道，則日失其度，暗昧亡光。

translations.literal: If the ruler does not cultivate the Way, then the sun loses its measure, dark and dim without light.
translations.idiomatic: Neglect the Way and the sun loses its course, dull and lightless.

sentences.zh: 各有雲為：其於東方作，日初出時，陰雲邪氣起者，法為牽於女謁，有所畏難；

translations.literal: Each has its cloud formation: when in the east it acts at the sun's first emergence, if yin clouds and evil vapor arise, the pattern is being dragged by women's pleas and having something feared and difficult;
translations.idiomatic: East at dawn: if dark clouds rise, the sign points to harem intrigue and hidden fear;

sentences.zh: 日出後，為近臣亂政；

translations.literal: after sunrise, it is near ministers disordering government;
translations.idiomatic: after sunrise, favorites meddle with rule;

sentences.zh: 日中，為大臣欺誣；

translations.literal: at noon, great ministers practicing deceit;
translations.idiomatic: at noon, high ministers lie;

sentences.zh: 日且入，為妻妾役使所營。

translations.literal: when the sun is about to enter, it is wives and concubines and servants managing affairs.
translations.idiomatic: near sunset, wives, concubines, and servants pull the strings.

sentences.zh: 間者日尤不精，光明侵奪失色，邪氣珥蜺數作。

translations.literal: Recently the sun has especially lacked purity; brightness suffered encroachment and seizure of color; evil vapor formed pendants and rainbows repeatedly.
translations.idiomatic: Of late the sun has sickened—its light eaten away, halos and mock-suns again and again.

sentences.zh: 本起於晨，相連至昏，其日出後至日中間差愈。

translations.literal: Originally arising at dawn, linked together until dusk; between its emergence after sunrise and noon the discrepancy grew worse.
translations.idiomatic: The smear begins at dawn and lasts till dusk, worst between sunrise and noon.

sentences.zh: 小臣不知內事，竊以日視陛下志操，衰於始初多矣。

translations.literal: Your petty servant does not know inner affairs; I have privately used the sun to view Your Majesty's intent and conduct—they have declined greatly from the beginning.
translations.idiomatic: I know nothing of the inner palace; I read only the sun—and it says your will has slipped far from what it was at first.

sentences.zh: 其咎恐有以守正直言而得罪者，傷嗣害世，不可不慎也。

translations.literal: The blame—I fear there are those who by guarding correctness and speaking straight received punishment—harming heirs and injuring generations; one cannot fail to be cautious.
translations.idiomatic: I fear straight men punished for honest words—that way lies hurt to the heir and ruin for ages. Beware.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下執乾剛之德，強志守度，毋聽女謁邪臣之態。

translations.literal: May Your Majesty hold fast the virtue of Qian's hardness, strengthen your intent and guard the measure, and not listen to the posture of women's pleas and deviant ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Cling to the firm virtue of Qian, hold your course, and deafen your ears to harem whispers and crooked ministers.

sentences.zh: 諸保阿乳母甘言悲辭之托，斷而勿聽。

translations.literal: As for the entreaties of nursemaids and wet nurses in sweet words and sorrowful phrases—cut them off and do not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Nurses and foster-mothers cooing pity—cut them off.

sentences.zh: 勉強大誼，絕小不忍；

translations.literal: Exert yourself on the great righteousness and cut off what small pity cannot bear;
translations.idiomatic: You must choose the great right over petty sentimental weakness.

sentences.zh: 良有不得已，可賜以貨財，不可私以官位，誠皇天之禁也。

translations.literal: when there is truly what cannot be helped, you may grant goods and wealth, but may not privately grant office and rank—this is truly what august Heaven forbids.
translations.idiomatic: When you must show favor, give gold, not posts—Heaven forbids the latter.

sentences.zh: 日失其光，則星辰放寬。

translations.literal: When the sun loses its light, then the stars run loose.
translations.idiomatic: Dim the sun and the stars wander where they should not.

sentences.zh: 陽不能制陰，陰桀得作。

translations.literal: Yang cannot control yin; yin in violence is able to act.
translations.idiomatic: When yang cannot check yin, yin rises in violence.

sentences.zh: 間者太白正晝經天。

translations.literal: Recently Great White in correct daylight crossed the sky.
translations.idiomatic: Venus has lately crossed the meridian in broad day.

sentences.zh: 宜隆德克躬，以執不軌。

translations.literal: It is fitting to heighten virtue and overcome oneself in the body, thereby to seize those who are not upright.
translations.idiomatic: Heap up inner virtue, master yourself, and seize traitors while you can.

sentences.zh: 臣聞月者，眾陰之長，銷息見伏，百里為品，千里立表，萬里連紀，妃後大臣諸侯之象也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the moon is chief of the many yin; it wanes, rests, appears, and hides—a hundred li sets the grade, a thousand li sets the marker, ten thousand li links the record—it is the image of consorts, great ministers, and feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: The moon governs yin; wax and wane mark fiefs near and far—emblem of empress, ministers, and princes.

sentences.zh: 朔晦正終始，弦為繩墨，望成君德，春夏南，秋冬北。

translations.literal: New moon and dark moon correct beginning and end; crescent is ink-line; full moon completes the ruler's virtue; spring and summer south, autumn and winter north.
translations.idiomatic: New and dark moons fix the cycle; first quarter is the plumb-line; full moon shows the king's virtue; spring and summer she rides south, autumn and winter north.

sentences.zh: 間者，月數以春夏與日同道，過軒轅上後受氣，入太微帝廷揚光輝，犯上將近臣，列星皆失色，厭厭如滅，此為母后與政亂朝，陰陽俱傷，兩不相便。

translations.literal: Recently the moon several times in spring and summer shared the road with the sun, passed above the Chariot Handle and afterward received vapor, entered Grand Tenuity's imperial court and raised radiance, encroached on the senior general and near ministers; the arrayed stars all lost color, fading as if extinguished—this is empress dowagers sharing government and disordering the court, yin and yang both injured, the two not benefiting each other.
translations.idiomatic: Lately the moon has run with the sun through spring and summer, crossed the Handle, entered the imperial asterism, glared on the chief general and inner circle—every star paled. That is empresses meddling in government, yin and yang wounding each other.

sentences.zh: 外臣不知朝事，竊信天文即如此，近臣已不足仗矣。

translations.literal: Outer ministers do not know court affairs; I have privately trusted the astronomy as thus—near ministers are already not enough to rely on.
translations.idiomatic: We outsiders cannot know the inner court; the sky says your intimates are no longer trustworthy.

sentences.zh: 屋大柱小，可為寒心。

translations.literal: A house with a great roof-post and small pillars—one may for this feel cold at heart.
translations.idiomatic: A broad roof on narrow timbers should freeze your heart.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下親求賢士，無強所惡，以崇社稷，尊強本朝。

translations.literal: May Your Majesty personally seek worthy gentlemen, not force what you detest, thereby to exalt the altars of soil and grain and strengthen the root of the court.
translations.idiomatic: Seek worthies yourself, do not fill posts with men you despise—raise the state and thicken the dynasty's root.

sentences.zh: 臣聞五星者，五行之精，五帝司命，應王者號令為之節度。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the five planets are the quintessence of the five phases, the five emperors' directors of fate, responding to the king's commands and setting their measures.
translations.idiomatic: The five planets embody the five agents and the five celestial emperors; they answer the king's edicts and set his calendar.

sentences.zh: 歲星主歲事，為統首，號令所紀，今失度而盛，此君指意欲有所為，未得其節也。

translations.literal: The Year Star governs the year's affairs; it is the head of the system, what commands and records depend on—now it has lost measure and is excessive; this is the ruler's intent wishing to do something but not yet obtaining its measure.
translations.idiomatic: Jupiter rules the year's business; now he strays and swells—the throne means to act but has not found the right pace.

sentences.zh: 又填星不避歲星者，後帝共政，相留於奎、婁，當以義斷之。

translations.literal: Moreover when Saturn does not yield to the Year Star, empresses share government together, mutually lingering in Kui and Lou—one should decide it by righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: Saturn will not give way to Jupiter—two powers share the sky in Kui and Lou; righteousness must cut the knot.

sentences.zh: 熒惑往來亡常，周歷兩宮，作態低卬，入天門，上明堂，貫尾亂宮。

translations.literal: Mars comes and goes without constancy, circling the two palaces, posturing high and low, entering Heaven's Gate, ascending Bright Hall, piercing the Tail and disordering the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Mars roams wild through the two celestial palaces, bowing and rearing, enters the Gate, climbs Bright Hall, stabs the Tail and disorders the harem.

sentences.zh: 太白髮越犯庫，兵寇之應也。

translations.literal: Great White bristling and encroaching on the arsenals is the response of weapons and bandits.
translations.idiomatic: Venus bristling over the armory-stars means arms and raiders.

sentences.zh: 貫黃龍，入帝庭，當門而出，隨熒惑入天門，至房而分，欲與熒惑為患，不敢當明堂之精。

translations.literal: It pierced the Yellow Dragon, entered the imperial court, went out at the gate, followed Mars into Heaven's Gate, reached Room and split—wishing to join Mars in disaster, not daring to match the quintessence of Bright Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Venus crossed the Yellow Dragon, entered the throne stars, followed Mars through the Gate, reached Room and forked—plotting mischief with Mars yet shrinking from Bright Hall's pure fire.

sentences.zh: 此陛下神靈，故禍亂不成也。

translations.literal: This is Your Majesty's numinous power; therefore disaster and disorder did not succeed.
translations.idiomatic: Your own spirit blocked the omen from ripening into coup.

sentences.zh: 熒惑厥弛，佞巧依勢，微言毀譽，進類蔽善。

translations.literal: Mars relaxes its bowstring; flattery and craft lean on power; subtle words of slander and praise advance their kind and screen the good.
translations.idiomatic: Mars slacks his string; slick men ride power, whisper slander, promote their clique, and smother the good.

sentences.zh: 太白出端門，臣有不臣者。

translations.literal: Great White emerging at the Duan Gate means there are ministers who are not ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Venus at the outer gate means subjects who mean to be more than subjects.

sentences.zh: 火入室，金上堂，不以時解，其憂凶。

translations.literal: Fire enters the chamber, metal ascends the hall—if not untied in due season, the worry is ill-omened.
translations.idiomatic: Fire in the inner room, metal in the hall—if not eased in time, the omen is deadly.

sentences.zh: 填、歲相守，又主內亂。

translations.literal: Saturn and the Year Star guarding each other also chiefly mean inner disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Jupiter and Saturn locked together spell civil strife.

sentences.zh: 宜察蕭牆之內，毋急親疏之微，誅放佞人，防絕萌牙，以蕩滌濁濊，消散積惡，毋使得成禍亂。

translations.literal: It is fitting to examine within the inner wall, not be hasty over the fine points of nearness and distance, execute and banish flatterers, guard and cut off sprouts and buds, thereby to wash away turbid filth, disperse accumulated evil, and not allow them to complete disaster and disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Search within your own walls; do not fuss over kin versus stranger—purge flatterers at the root, scour filth, scatter stored-up vice before it flowers into revolt.

sentences.zh: 辰星主正四時，當效於四仲；

translations.literal: The Chronograph Star governs correcting the four seasons; it ought to appear at the four midmonths;
translations.idiomatic: Mercury should appear at each season's midpoint to set the calendar;

sentences.zh: 四時失序，則辰星作異。

translations.literal: when the four seasons lose their order, then the Chronograph Star makes anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: when seasons slip, Mercury acts strange.

sentences.zh: 今出於歲首之孟，天所以譴告陛下也。

translations.literal: Now it has emerged in the first month of the year's beginning—Heaven thereby reproves and warns Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: Now it has shown in the first month of the year—Heaven's rebuke to you.

sentences.zh: 政急則出早，政緩則出晚，政絕不行則伏不見而為彗□。

translations.literal: When government is urgent it emerges early; when government is slack it emerges late; when government is cut off and not practiced then it hides and does not appear and becomes a comet [lacuna].
translations.idiomatic: Harsh rule brings it early, lax rule late; dead policy hides it until it bursts as a comet—the text is damaged here.

sentences.zh: 四孟皆出，為易王命；

translations.literal: If it emerges in all four first months, it means the kingly mandate changes;
translations.idiomatic: Appear in all four opening months and the mandate shifts;

sentences.zh: 四季皆出，星家所諱。

translations.literal: if it emerges in all four last months, it is what star diviners taboo.
translations.idiomatic: appear in all four closing months and astrologers turn pale.

sentences.zh: 今幸獨出寅孟之月，蓋皇天所以篤右陛下也，宜深自改。

translations.literal: Now fortunately it alone emerged in the first month of yin—this is how august Heaven thickly aids Your Majesty; it is fitting deeply to reform yourself.
translations.idiomatic: It has shown only in yin's first month—Heaven still singles you out for favor; reform yourself deeply.

sentences.zh: 治國故不可以戚戚，欲速則不達。

translations.literal: In governing the state one therefore cannot be anxious and anxious; desiring speed, one does not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Rule cannot be driven by panic; haste misses the mark.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「三載考績，三考黜陟。」

translations.literal: The classic says, 'In three years examine merit; in three examinations demote and promote.'
translations.idiomatic: The canon says: three years to test merit, three tests to raise or fall.

sentences.zh: 加以號令不順四時，既往不咎，來事之師也。

translations.literal: Add to this that commands and edicts have not followed the four seasons—what is past is not blamed; it is the teacher of what is to come.
translations.idiomatic: Edicts out of season are a fault of the past—let them instruct the future, not draw blame.

sentences.zh: 間者春三月治大獄，時賊陰立逆，恐歲小收；

translations.literal: Recently in the third month of spring great prisons were tried; at that time wicked yin stood contrary—I fear the year's harvest will be small.
translations.idiomatic: Great spring trials fed yin against yang—expect a thin harvest.

sentences.zh: 季夏舉兵法，時寒氣應，恐後有霜雹之災；

translations.literal: In late summer military law was raised; at that time cold vapor responded—I fear afterward there will be disaster of frost and hail.
translations.idiomatic: Military drills in late summer drew cold—expect frost and hail.

sentences.zh: 秋月行封爵，其月土濕奧，恐後有雷雹之變。

translations.literal: In autumn moon enfeoffments were carried out; that month earth was damp and muggy—I fear afterward there will be change of thunder and hail.
translations.idiomatic: Autumn enfeoffments in a muggy month invite thunder and hail.

sentences.zh: 夫以喜怒賞罰，而不顧時禁，雖有堯、舜之心，猶不能致和。

translations.literal: To reward and punish by joy and anger without regard to seasonal prohibitions—even with the hearts of Yao and Shun, still one cannot bring harmony.
translations.idiomatic: Reward and punish by mood, ignore the seasons—even Yao and Shun could not keep harmony.

sentences.zh: 善言天者，必有效於人。

translations.literal: Those good at speaking of Heaven must have effect on man.
translations.idiomatic: He who reads the sky must show its lesson in human affairs.

sentences.zh: 設上農夫而欲冬田，肉袒深耕，汗出種之，然猶不生者，非人心不至，天時不得也。

translations.literal: Suppose there were a supreme farmer who wished to winter-plow, baring his shoulders and plowing deep, sweating to sow the seed—yet still it would not grow; it is not that human intent did not reach, but the season of Heaven was not obtained.
translations.idiomatic: The best farmer cannot force a winter crop—intent is not enough if Heaven withholds the hour.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「時止則止，時行則行，動靜不失其時，其道光明。」

translations.literal: The Changes says, 'When the time stops, then stop; when the time goes, then go; movement and rest do not miss their time—then the Way is bright.'
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: stop when time stops, move when it moves—never miss the moment, and the Way shines.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「敬授民時。」

translations.literal: The Documents says, 'Reverently give the people their seasons.'
translations.idiomatic: The Documents says: reverently hand the people their seasons.

sentences.zh: 故古之王者，尊天地，重陰陽，敬四時，嚴月令。

translations.literal: Therefore the kings of antiquity honored Heaven and Earth, valued yin and yang, revered the four seasons, and were strict about monthly ordinances.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient kings honored heaven and earth, weighed yin and yang, revered the four seasons, and kept the monthly rules.

sentences.zh: 順之以善政，則和氣可立致，猶□鼓之相應也。

translations.literal: If one follows them with good government, then harmonizing vapor can be established immediately—it is like [lacuna] drum answering each other.
translations.idiomatic: Good policy tracks the months as mallet answers drum—the text marks a damaged graph.

sentences.zh: 今朝廷忽於時月之令，諸侍中、尚書近臣宜皆令通知月令之意，設群下請事；

translations.literal: Now the court neglects the commands of seasons and months; the various palace attendants and secretaries of the masters of writing who are near ministers should all be ordered to comprehend the meaning of monthly ordinances, and when the host of subordinates request affairs;
translations.idiomatic: The court neglects seasonal edicts; every attendant and secretary should learn the monthly ordinances;

sentences.zh: 若陛下出令有謬於時者，當知爭之，以順時氣。

translations.literal: if Your Majesty issues a command that errs against the season, they ought to know to dispute it, thereby to comply with seasonal vapor.
translations.idiomatic: when your order breaks the calendar, they must object—to keep the year's breath true.

sentences.zh: 臣聞五行以水為本，其星玄武婺女，天地所紀，終始所生。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that in the five phases water is the root; its star is Dark Warrior and Maiden—the reckoning of Heaven and Earth, what beginning and end engender.
translations.idiomatic: Among the five agents water is root; its asterism is the Dark Warrior and Maid—Heaven and Earth's tally, source of all cycles.

sentences.zh: 水為準平，王道公正修明，則百川理，落脈通；

translations.literal: Water is leveling and even; when the kingly Way is fair, upright, cultivated, and bright, then the hundred streams are ordered and falling veins are open;
translations.idiomatic: Water seeks the level—when the Way is just, rivers run true and channels stay clear;

sentences.zh: 偏黨失綱，則踴溢為敗。

translations.literal: when partiality and faction lose the net, then they bubble over in defeat.
translations.idiomatic: when bias snaps the net, they boil over in ruin.

sentences.zh: 《書》云「水曰潤下」，陰動而卑，不失其道。

translations.literal: The Documents says 'Water is called moistening downward'—yin moves and is low, not missing its Way.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents calls water 'the soaking downward force'—yin moves low and keeps its course.

sentences.zh: 天下有道，則河出圖，洛出書，故河、洛決溢，所為最大。

translations.literal: When there is the Way in the realm, then the Yellow River yields the chart and the Luo yields the writing—therefore when the Yellow River and Luo overflow, what they do is greatest.
translations.idiomatic: When the Way prevails, the Yellow River brings forth the chart and the Luo the writing—so when those rivers burst their banks, the damage is worst of all.

sentences.zh: 今汝、穎畎澮皆川水漂踴，與雨水並為民害，此《詩》所謂「燁燁震電，不寧不令，百川沸騰」者也。

translations.literal: Now the ditches and canals of Ru and Ying are all river water surging and leaping, together with rain water becoming harm to the people—this is what the Odes calls 'Flashing, flashing, the shaking lightning—not good, not auspicious; the hundred streams boil up.'
translations.idiomatic: The channels of Ru and Ying now run wild with river flood on top of rain—exactly the Odes' 'Glaring lightning, ill omens all around, every stream boiling up.'

sentences.zh: 其咎在於皇甫卿士之屬。

translations.literal: The blame lies in the class of Grand Steward Huangfu.
translations.idiomatic: The fault lies with favorites like Grand Steward Huangfu.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下留意詩人之言，少抑外親大臣。

translations.literal: May Your Majesty heed the poet's words and slightly restrain outer kin and great ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Heed the classic poets, sire, and rein in your in-laws and great ministers a little.

sentences.zh: 臣聞地道柔靜，陰之常義也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that the Way of earth is soft and still—the constant meaning of yin.
translations.idiomatic: Earth's nature is soft and still—that is yin's constant law.

sentences.zh: 地有上、中、下：其上位震，應妃、後不順；

translations.literal: Earth has upper, middle, and lower: its upper position quakes, responding to consorts and empresses who are not compliant;
translations.idiomatic: The earth's upper zone trembles when consorts and empresses defy order.

sentences.zh: 中位應大臣作亂；

translations.literal: the middle position responds to great ministers making rebellion;
translations.idiomatic: Its middle zone shakes when great ministers plot rebellion.

sentences.zh: 下位應庶民離畔。

translations.literal: the lower position responds to commoners leaving their allegiance.
translations.idiomatic: Its lower zone shakes when the common people turn disloyal.

sentences.zh: 震或於其國，國君之咎也。

translations.literal: When there is quake perhaps in that state, it is the fault of the state's lord.
translations.idiomatic: A quake centered in one state is that ruler's fault.

sentences.zh: 四方中央連國歷州俱動者，其異最大。

translations.literal: When four quarters, center, linked states, and successive provinces all move together, that anomaly is greatest.
translations.idiomatic: When every region and province trembles as one, the omen is gravest.

sentences.zh: 間者關東地數震，五星作異，亦未大逆，宜務崇陽抑陰，以救其咎；

translations.literal: Recently east of the Pass the earth has several times quaked, and the five planets have made anomalies—still not greatly contrary; it is fitting to strive to honor yang and check yin, thereby to save this blame;
translations.idiomatic: The east has quaked repeatedly and the five wanderers act strange—yet all is not lost: exalt yang, damp yin, and answer Heaven's rebuke;

sentences.zh: 固志建威，閉絕私路，拔進英雋，退不任職，以強本朝。

translations.literal: firm intent and establish awesomeness, block off private byways, raise and advance heroic excellence, retire those not fit for office—thereby to strengthen this court.
translations.idiomatic: steel your will, shut private shortcuts, promote the able, cashier the useless, and thicken the court's spine.

sentences.zh: 夫本強則精神折衝，本弱則招殃致凶，為邪謀所陵。

translations.literal: When the root is strong then spirit and vapor repel assailants; when the root is weak then it invites disaster and brings ill fortune, to be overrun by wicked plots.
translations.idiomatic: A strong root turns back malice; a weak root invites plotters to trample you.

sentences.zh: 聞往者淮南王作謀之時，其所難者，獨有汲黯，以為公孫弘等不足言也。

translations.literal: I have heard that when the king of Huainan plotted rebellion, the one he feared was only Ji An; he thought Gongsun Hong and the like not worth mentioning.
translations.idiomatic: When Huainan plotted revolt, he feared only Ji An; men like Gongsun Hong he dismissed.

sentences.zh: 弘，漢之名相，於今亡比，而尚見輕，何況亡弘之屬乎？

translations.literal: Hong was Han's famous chancellor; compared with today there is no peer—yet still he was held light; how much more those who are not Hong's class!
translations.idiomatic: Hong was a famous Han chancellor—none today match him—yet plotters sneered at him; what of lesser men?

sentences.zh: 故曰朝廷亡人，則為賊亂所輕，其道自然也。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: when the court has no men, then it is held light by bandits and disorder—its Way is naturally so.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: an empty court invites contempt from every rebel—that is nature's rule.

sentences.zh: 天下未聞陛下奇策固守之臣也。

translations.literal: The realm has not heard of Your Majesty's marvelous strategists and ministers who hold fast in defense.
translations.idiomatic: The empire has yet to hear of ministers who guard your throne with singular strategy.

sentences.zh: 語曰，何以知朝廷之衰？

translations.literal: A saying asks, how does one know the court is declining?
translations.idiomatic: There is an old test for a failing court:

sentences.zh: 人人自賢，不務於通人，故世陵夷。

translations.literal: Each man deems himself worthy and does not strive to communicate with others—therefore generations decline.
translations.idiomatic: every man calls himself wise and will not learn from another—then the age slides downhill.

sentences.zh: 馬不伏歷，不可以趨道；

translations.literal: If the horse has not submitted to the bit, it cannot be driven on the road;
translations.idiomatic: A horse not broken to the curb cannot keep the road;

sentences.zh: 士不素養，不可以重國。

translations.literal: if the gentleman has not been trained in advance, he cannot be relied on to weight the state.
translations.idiomatic: a scholar not long trained cannot bear the state's weight.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰「濟濟多士，文王以寧」，孔子曰「十室之邑，必有忠信」，非虛言也。

translations.literal: The Odes says, 'Many were the officers, and King Wen had tranquility by them'; Confucius said, 'In a hamlet of ten houses there must be those who are loyal and trustworthy'—these are not empty words.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes praises King Wen's many officers; Confucius said even ten roofs hide loyalty—these are not idle boasts.

sentences.zh: 陛下秉四海之眾，曾亡柱干之固守聞於四境，殆聞之不廣，取之不明，勸之不篤，傳曰：「士之美者善養禾，君之明者善養士。」

translations.literal: Your Majesty holds the multitude of the four seas—yet there is none heard of pillar and shield holding fast on the borders; perhaps hearing is not broad, selection is not clear, encouragement is not earnest. The tradition says, 'The beautiful among gentlemen are good at nurturing grain; the enlightened among lords are good at nurturing gentlemen.'
translations.idiomatic: You command the four seas, yet no champion of the borders makes his name heard—perhaps you listen too narrowly, choose too dimly, urge too coldly. The saying runs: good farmers tend the crop; wise kings tend the men.

sentences.zh: 中人皆可使為君子。

translations.literal: Men of middling quality may all be made gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: Even middling talents can be shaped into gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 詔書進賢良，赦小過，無求備，以博聚英雋。

translations.literal: The edict advances the worthy and good, pardons small faults, seeks not completeness, thereby broadly to gather heroic excellence.
translations.idiomatic: Your edict calls the worthy, forgives small slips, asks no perfection—so gather talent widely.

sentences.zh: 如近世貢禹，以言事忠切蒙尊榮，當此之時，士厲身立名者多。

translations.literal: Such as in recent generations Gong Yu, because his words on affairs were loyal and cutting, received honor and glory—at that time many among the gentlemen exerted themselves to establish name.
translations.idiomatic: When Gong Yu spoke blunt truth and won glory, many scholars strove to make a name.

sentences.zh: 禹死之後，日日以衰。

translations.literal: After Yu died, day by day it declined.
translations.idiomatic: After Gong Yu died, the courage drained away day by day.

sentences.zh: 及京兆尹王章坐言事誅滅，智者結舌，邪偽並興，外戚顓命，君臣隔塞，至絕繼嗣，女宮作亂。

translations.literal: When metropolitan governor Wang Zhang sat guilty of words on affairs and was executed and extinguished, the wise sealed their tongues, the false and wicked rose together, outer kin monopolized commands, lord and minister were blocked and sealed off, until heirs were cut off and the ladies' palace made disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Then Wang Zhang died for memorializing—the wise fell silent, knaves multiplied, in-laws seized the voice of power, ruler and minister stopped hearing each other, the line nearly died, and the harem brewed revolt.

sentences.zh: 此行事之敗，誠可畏而悲也。

translations.literal: This is the defeat of conduct in affairs—truly to be feared and mourned.
translations.idiomatic: That is how policy fails—terrifying and pitiful.

sentences.zh: 本在積任母后之家，非一日之漸，往者不可及，來者猶可追也。

translations.literal: The root lies in long accumulation of trusting the empress dowager's houses—not a single day's gradualness; what is past cannot be reached, what comes still may be pursued.
translations.idiomatic: The rot came from long favor to maternal clans—not overnight; the past is gone, the future can still be mended.

sentences.zh: 先帝大聖，深見天意昭然，使陛下奉承天統，欲矯正之也。

translations.literal: The late emperor was greatly sage, deeply seeing Heaven's intent clear and bright, causing Your Majesty to inherit Heaven's succession, wishing to correct it.
translations.idiomatic: The late emperor read Heaven's will clearly and left you the succession to set it right.

sentences.zh: 宜少抑外親，選練左右，舉有德行道術通明之士充備天官，然後可以輔聖德，保帝位，承大宗。

translations.literal: It is fitting slightly to restrain outer kin, select and drill those at left and right, raise men possessed of virtue, conduct, arts, and thorough enlightenment to fill the offices of Heaven—then one can assist sagely virtue, secure the imperial seat, and inherit the great lineage.
translations.idiomatic: Curb the in-laws, drill your attendants, fill heaven's offices with men of clear virtue and learning—then you aid the sage virtue, secure the throne, and carry the great line.

sentences.zh: 下至郎吏從官，行能亡以異，又不通一藝，及博士無文雅者，宜皆使就南畝，以視天下，明朝廷皆賢材君子，於以重朝尊君，滅凶致安，此其本也。

translations.literal: Down to gentlemen of the palace and followers, if conduct and ability show no distinction, and again they do not master one art, and among erudites there are none with literary refinement—all should be sent to the southern fields, to show the realm that the court is all worthy material and gentlemen—thereby to weight the court and honor the ruler, extinguish the ill-omened and bring peace—this is the root.
translations.idiomatic: Even petty clerks who show no talent and master no single art, and erudites without polish—send them back to the plough; let the world see the court stuffed with true men—that steadies the throne and ends disaster.

sentences.zh: 臣自知所言害身，不辟死亡之誅，唯財留神，反覆復愚臣之言。

translations.literal: Your servant knows by himself that what he speaks harms his person; I do not avoid execution for death—only may Your Majesty fix your spirit and turn over again this foolish servant's words.
translations.idiomatic: I know this speech may cost my life—I do not shun death; only fix your mind, sire, and read my foolish words twice.

sentences.zh: 是時，哀帝初立，成帝外家王氏未甚抑黜，而帝外家丁、傅新貴，祖母傅太后尤驕恣，欲稱尊號。

translations.literal: At this time Emperor Ai had newly ascended; the Wang clan, outer kin of Emperor Cheng, had not yet been much demoted, while Your Majesty's outer kin Ding and Fu were newly honored, and Grand Empress Dowager Fu especially was arrogant and wanton, wishing to assume a lofty title.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ai had just taken the throne: the Wangs were not yet cut back, while the Ding and Fu houses rose, and Grand Empress Dowager Fu demanded an exalted style.

sentences.zh: 丞相孔光、大司空師丹執政諫爭，久之，上不得已，遂免光、丹而尊傅太后。

translations.literal: Chancellor Kong Guang and grand minister of works Shi Dan held government and remonstrated and contended; after a long time the sovereign had no choice, thereupon dismissed Guang and Dan and honored Grand Empress Dowager Fu.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Guang and Shi Dan fought the title in council; at last the emperor gave way—he cashiered both ministers and granted Fu her grand style.

sentences.zh: 語在《丹傳》。

translations.literal: The words are in the 'Biography of Dan.'
translations.idiomatic: The details stand in Dan's biography.

sentences.zh: 上雖不從尋言，然采其語，每有非常，輒問尋。

translations.literal: Although the sovereign did not follow Xun's words, yet he adopted his language—whenever there was something extraordinary, he always questioned Xun.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor spurned Xun's larger advice but kept his lore—at every strange omen he called Xun in.

sentences.zh: 尋對屢中，遷黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: Xun's replies repeatedly hit the mark; he was promoted to gentleman of the yellow gates.
translations.idiomatic: Xun's readings came true again and again; he rose to gentleman of the yellow gates.

sentences.zh: 以尋言且有水災，故拜尋為騎都尉，使護河堤。

translations.literal: Because Xun's words said there would soon be flood disaster, therefore Xun was appointed chief of cavalry and sent to protect the river dikes.
translations.idiomatic: Expecting flood from Xun's word, the court named him chief of cavalry to oversee the dikes.

sentences.zh: 初，成帝時，齊人甘忠可詐造《天官曆》、《包元太平經》十二卷，以言「漢家逢天地之大終，當更受命於天，天帝使真人赤精子，下教我此道。」

translations.literal: Originally in the time of Emperor Cheng, a man of Qi, Gan Zhongke, forged in fraud the Celestial Offices Calendar and the twelve scrolls of the Primordial Origin Great Peace Classic, saying, 'The house of Han has met Heaven and Earth's great terminus and ought to receive renewed mandate from Heaven; the High God sent the True Man Red Essence Lord down to teach your servant this Way.'
translations.idiomatic: Under Cheng, Gan Zhongke of Qi forged the Celestial Calendar and a twelve-scroll Great Peace scripture, claiming Han had reached cosmic year's end and must take a new mandate—the Red Essence True Man had taught him so.

sentences.zh: 忠可以教重平夏賀良、容丘丁廣世、東郡郭昌等，中壘校尉劉向奏忠可假鬼神罔上惑眾，下獄治服，未斷病死。

translations.literal: Zhongke taught Zhongping Xia Liang of Xia, Ding Guangshi of Rongqiu, Guo Chang of Dong commandery, and others; colonel of the inner ramparts Liu Xiang memorialized that Zhongke had falsely invoked ghosts and spirits to deceive those above and delude the multitude; he was cast into prison and confessed under treatment, and before sentence died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: He taught disciples until Liu Xiang had him jailed for feigning spirits to fool the court; he confessed under torture and died before sentencing.

sentences.zh: 賀良等坐挾學忠可書以不敬論，後賀良等復私以相教。

translations.literal: Xia Liang and the rest sat guilty of clutching Zhongke's writings to study, judged irreverent; afterward Xia Liang and the rest again privately taught one another.
translations.idiomatic: His pupils were fined for studying the forbidden book, then went on teaching it in secret.

sentences.zh: 哀帝初立，司隸校尉解光亦以明經通災異得幸，白賀良等所挾忠可書。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ai first ascended, colonel of the metropolitan region Xie Guang, also by mastery of the classics and understanding portents and anomalies, won favor, and reported the Zhongke writings that Xia Liang and the rest clutched.
translations.idiomatic: At Ai's accession Xie Guang, skilled in classics and omens, denounced the same scriptures.

sentences.zh: 事下奉車都尉劉歆，歆以為不合《五經》，不可施行。

translations.literal: The matter was sent down to chief of the imperial equipage Liu Xin; Xin held that it did not accord with the Five Classics and could not be practiced.
translations.idiomatic: The case went to Liu Xin, who ruled it contradicted the Five Classics and could not be used.

sentences.zh: 而李尋亦好之。

translations.literal: But Li Xun also loved it.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xun, however, favored the teaching.

sentences.zh: 光曰：「前歆父向奏忠可下獄，歆安肯通此道？」

translations.literal: Guang said, 'Formerly Xin's father Xiang memorialized to cast Zhongke into prison—how could Xin be willing to open this Way?'
translations.idiomatic: Xie Guang said, 'Xin's own father jailed Gan Zhongke—would Xin embrace that doctrine?'

sentences.zh: 時，郭昌為長安令，勸尋宜助賀良等。

translations.literal: At the time Guo Chang was magistrate of Chang'an and urged Xun that it was fitting to assist Xia Liang and the rest.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Chang, magistrate of Chang'an, urged Xun to back Xia Liang.

sentences.zh: 尋遂白賀良等皆待詔黃門，數詔見，陳說：「漢歷中衰，當更受命。

translations.literal: Xun thereupon reported that Xia Liang and the rest should all await edicts at the yellow gates; several times they were summoned and saw the sovereign, stating, 'Han calendar is in middle decline; it ought to receive renewed mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Xun then had them posted as yellow-gate expectants; they won audiences and declared Han's calendar spent and due for renewal.

sentences.zh: 成帝不應天命，故絕嗣。

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng did not answer Heaven's mandate; therefore heirs were cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Cheng defied Heaven—so his line failed.

sentences.zh: 今陛下久疾，變異屢數，天所以譴告人也。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty has long been ill; changes and anomalies have repeatedly occurred—this is how Heaven reproves and warns mankind.
translations.idiomatic: Your long illness and the string of portents are Heaven scolding mankind.

sentences.zh: 宜急改元易號，乃得延年益壽，皇子生，災異息矣。

translations.literal: It is fitting urgently to change the era-name and alter the style—then you can prolong years and increase longevity, a princely son will be born, and portents and anomalies will rest.
translations.idiomatic: Change the era title and the court style at once—then you will live longer, get an heir, and the omens will stop.

sentences.zh: 得道不得行，咎殃且亡，不有洪水將出，災火且起，滌蕩民人。」

translations.literal: If one obtains the Way but cannot practice it, blame and disaster will soon arrive; if not, there will be great flood about to issue, disaster fire about to rise, washing and scourging the people.'
translations.idiomatic: Hold the doctrine but refuse the deed, and calamity follows—flood and cleansing fire will scour the people.'

sentences.zh: 後月餘，上疾自若。

translations.literal: After more than a month the sovereign's illness was as before.
translations.idiomatic: A month passed; the emperor was as sick as ever.

sentences.zh: 賀良等復欲妄變政事，大臣爭以為不可許。

translations.literal: Xia Liang and the rest again wished rashly to change government affairs; the great ministers contended that it could not be permitted.
translations.idiomatic: Xia Liang tried to reorder the government; the ministers blocked him.

sentences.zh: 賀良等奏言大臣皆不知天命，宜退丞相御史，以解光、李尋輔政。

translations.literal: Xia Liang and the rest memorialized, saying the great ministers all did not know Heaven's mandate and it was fitting to retire the chancellor and imperial counsellor and have Xie Guang and Li Xun assist government.
translations.idiomatic: They demanded the chancellor and imperial counsellor be dismissed for ignorance of Heaven and replaced by Xie Guang and Li Xun.

sentences.zh: 上以其言亡驗，遂下賀良等吏，而下詔曰：「朕獲保宗廟，為政不德，變異屢仍，恐懼戰慄，未知所繇。

translations.literal: The sovereign because their words had no verification, thereupon sent Xia Liang and the rest down to the clerks, and issued an edict, saying, 'We have been able to preserve the ancestral temples; in governing We lack virtue; changes and anomalies have repeatedly continued; We fear and tremble, not knowing whence they come.
translations.idiomatic: Their prophecy failed; the emperor jailed them and wrote: 'I have kept the shrines yet lack virtue; omens pile up and I tremble, not knowing their source.

sentences.zh: 待詔賀良等建言改元易號，增益漏刻，可以永安國家。

translations.literal: Expectant edict Xia Liang and the rest proposed changing the era-name and altering the style, increasing the clepsydra's gradations, whereby the state could be forever secure.
translations.idiomatic: The expectants said new era name, new style, longer water-clock would save the state.

sentences.zh: 朕信道不篤，過聽其言，幾為百姓獲福。

translations.literal: We trusted the Way not firmly and wrongly listened to their words, nearly obtaining blessing for the hundred surnames.
translations.idiomatic: I trusted too lightly and nearly brought the people a blessing—

sentences.zh: 卒無嘉應，久旱為災。

translations.literal: In the end there was no fine response; long drought became disaster.
translations.idiomatic: —but no blessing came; drought followed instead.

sentences.zh: 以問賀良等，對當復改制度，皆背經誼，違聖制，不合時宜。

translations.literal: We questioned Xia Liang and the rest; they replied that one ought again to change institutions—all turned their backs on the classics' meaning, violated sage institutions, and did not fit the times.
translations.idiomatic: Questioned again, they demanded more institutional upheaval—every item breached the classics and sage precedent.

sentences.zh: 夫過而不改，是為過矣。

translations.literal: To have fault and not reform is to make fault.
translations.idiomatic: Fault uncorrected is doubled fault.

sentences.zh: 六月甲子詔書，非赦令，它皆蠲除之。

translations.literal: The jiazi edict of the sixth month was not an amnesty order; all other items are remitted and removed.
translations.idiomatic: The sixth month's jiazi edict was no general pardon—everything in it but the amnesty is void.

sentences.zh: 賀良等反道惑眾，奸態當窮竟。」

translations.literal: Xia Liang and the rest opposed the Way and deluded the multitude; wicked posture ought to be fully investigated.'
translations.idiomatic: Xia Liang and his party perverted the Way and misled the people—let their crime be fully tried.'

sentences.zh: 皆下獄，光祿勳平當、光祿大夫毛莫如與御史中丞、廷尉雜治，當賀良等執左道，亂朝政，傾覆國家，誣罔主上，不道。
