translations.literal: All were cast into prison; superintendent of the imperial household Ping Dang, grandee of the palace Mao Moruo, together with the imperial counsellor's vice director and commandant of justice jointly tried them, judging Xia Liang and the rest for holding the left-hand Way, disordering court government, overturning the state, and deceiving the sovereign above—lack of the Way.
translations.idiomatic: All went to prison; Ping Dang, Mao Moruo, and the judges found them guilty of left-hand doctrine, sedition, ruining the state, and deceiving the throne.

sentences.zh: 賀良等皆伏誅。

translations.literal: Xia Liang and the rest were all executed.
translations.idiomatic: Xia Liang and his fellows were executed.

sentences.zh: 尋及解光減死一等，徙敦煌郡。

translations.literal: Xun and Xie Guang had death reduced one degree and were banished to Dunhuang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xun and Xie Guang had sentence commuted one step—to exile in Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：幽贊神明，通合天人之道者，莫著乎《易》、《春秋》。

translations.literal: The eulogy says: Those who silently assist the numinous spirits and penetrate and join the Way of Heaven and man—nothing shows this more clearly than the Changes and Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: The summation runs: no texts join Heaven and man like the Changes and the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 然子贛猶云「夫子之文章可得而聞，夫子之言性與天道不可得而聞」已矣。

translations.literal: Yet Zigong still said, 'The Master's culture may be heard; the Master's words on nature and the Way of Heaven may not be heard'—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Zigong said we might learn the Master's culture but not his teaching on nature and Heaven—that was all he claimed.

sentences.zh: 漢興，推陰陽言災異者，孝武時有董仲舒、夏侯始昌；

translations.literal: When Han arose, those who pushed yin and yang and spoke portents and anomalies: under Emperor Wu the Martial there were Dong Zhongshu and Xiahou Shichang;
translations.idiomatic: Han omens-masters began with Dong Zhongshu and Xiahou Shichang under Emperor Wu;

sentences.zh: 昭、宣則眭孟、夏侯勝；

translations.literal: under Zhao and Xuan, Sui Meng and Xiahou Sheng;
translations.idiomatic: under Zhao and Xuan came Sui Meng and Xiahou Sheng;

sentences.zh: 元、成則京房、翼奉、劉向、谷永；

translations.literal: under Yuan and Cheng, Jing Fang, Ji Feng, Liu Xiang, and Gu Yong;
translations.idiomatic: Emperors Yuan and Cheng heard Jing Fang, Ji Feng, Liu Xiang, and Gu Yong.

sentences.zh: 哀、平則李尋、田終術。

translations.literal: under Ai and Ping, Li Xun and Tian Zhongshu.
translations.idiomatic: The last pair, under Ai and Ping, were Li Xun and Tian Zhongshu.

sentences.zh: 此其納說時君著明者也。

translations.literal: These are they whose persuasions to the rulers of the time were most clear.
translations.idiomatic: These were the ones whose counsel to their emperors rang clearest.

sentences.zh: 察其所言，彷彿一端。

translations.literal: Examining what they said, it seems one-sided on one end.
translations.idiomatic: Read their memorials and you find each seized one corner of truth.

sentences.zh: 假經設誼，依托象類，或不免乎「億則屢中」。

translations.literal: Borrowing the classics they set forth meanings, relying on images and categories—perhaps not free of 'guessing and often hitting.'
translations.idiomatic: They draped guesswork in classical garb and hit often enough by luck.

sentences.zh: 仲舒下吏，夏侯囚執，眭孟誅戮，李尋流放，此學者之大戒也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu was sent down to the clerks, Xiahou was imprisoned and seized, Sui Meng was executed, Li Xun was banished flowing—these are the great warnings to scholars.
translations.idiomatic: Dong fell to jail, Xiahou to chains, Sui Meng to the block, Li Xun to exile—object lessons for scholars.

sentences.zh: 京房區區，不量淺深，危言刺譏，樞怨強臣，罪辜不旋踵，亦不密以失身，悲夫！

translations.literal: Jing Fang was petty and small, not measuring shallow and deep, speaking perilous words and stabbing satire, knotting resentment with a strong minister—guilt and crime without turning the heel, also not secretive and thereby losing his person—alas!
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang was a small man who gauged neither depth nor danger, mocked power, and earned a strong minister's hate—dead before his foot could pivot, never learning silence—pitiful!
